bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Lorsque artisanal miners must meet labor and environnemental standards to access global markets, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unregulated extraction s'affaiblit.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking par mine environnemental and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Partiellement cassable. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal exploitation minière with proper safety and environnemental management can reduce dépendance du système compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report environnemental or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): Mettre en œuvre full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
Adopter IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
Investir dans formalization programs for artisanal exploitation minière communities
Ministry of Mines: Appliquer 2018 Mining Code environnemental provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: Renforcer mineral certification to include environnemental and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal exploitation minière registration and oversight
Contactez votre Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement pour exiger enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code environnemental provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
Plaider via the African Union for continental responsible exploitation minière standards
Pousser pour OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale exploitation minière. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping exploitation minière sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees exploitation minière policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for exploitation minière sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent économie ($15.7B PIB) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the intérêt chevauchant between international operators and government revenue s'affaiblit — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Lorsque non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Mettre en œuvre EITI-standard per-block revenue and émissions disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to environnemental data makes W computable per production unit.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report environnemental violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Mettre en œuvre Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Développer economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Adopter per-block émissions reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Renforcer environnemental conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include environnemental impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Plaider via CEMAC for regional environnemental standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with environnemental metrics
Pousser pour Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest économie in francophone West Africa ($87.1B PIB). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of PIB with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require déforestation-free supply chains — when per-farm déforestation data is linked to cocoa transactions, W devient calculable.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Lorsque cocoa buyers must verify zero-déforestation, the intérêt chevauchant between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm environnemental data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Partiellement cassable. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal déforestation for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Mettre en œuvre full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Investir dans cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Adopter living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Appliquer traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to environnemental compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Renforcer enforcement against déforestation in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Contactez votre Député in the Assemblée nationale pour exiger enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Plaider via the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding déforestation targets
Pousser pour living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end déforestation in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and environnemental regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to déforestation after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income économie ($330.3B PIB) with massive exploitation minière sector (16.23% of PIB). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium exploitation minière depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Lorsque par mine water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W devient calculable from exploitation minière output data.
bp: Mettre en œuvre water pricing that reflects true scarcity in exploitation minière regions. Lorsque miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, l'intérêt chevauchant dans water-intensive extraction methods s'affaiblit — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory par mine water balance, tailings chemistry, and émissions disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects environnemental data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report environnemental violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's lanceur d'alerte law (Ley 21.015) and environnemental complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Adopter Science Based Targets for émissions reduction across exploitation minière operations
Mettre en œuvre ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase environnemental monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at exploitation minière operations
Sernageomin: Renforcer tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Mettre en œuvre the National Lithium Strategy with binding environnemental standards
Contactez votre Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional pour exiger mandatory water-neutral exploitation minière operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on environnemental access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for exploitation minière supply chains
Pousser pour international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's environnemental enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with environnemental permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts environnemental impact assessments for exploitation minière projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's d'État copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper exploitation minière companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and exploitation minière service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees exploitation minière policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African économie ($53.3B PIB) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% PIB — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the intérêt chevauchant between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue s'affaiblit — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Lorsque REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued extraction breaks.
Mettre en œuvre EITI-standard per-block environnemental disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to environnemental data makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report environnemental violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Développerment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Mettre en œuvre environnemental monitoring and émissions reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Développer post-oil diversification strategy
Adopter TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Renforcer environnemental conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Appliquer environnemental impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to environnemental data

Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Plaider via CEMAC for regional environnemental standards
Pousser pour EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees exploitation minière and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American économie ($418.8B PIB) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, l'intérêt chevauchant dans fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in énergie renouvelable. Lorsque energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory par gisement émissions disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to émissions makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report environnemental violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
Mettre en œuvre TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
Investir dans énergie renouvelable in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: Renforcer environnemental licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: Réformer energy tariffs to accelerate énergie renouvelable deployment
ANH: Mettre en œuvre par gisement émissions monitoring
Contactez votre Representante or Senador in the Congreso to support the energy transition framework legislation
Renforcer Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral émissions reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance environnemental governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's environnemental licensing authority, responsible for environnemental impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% d'État), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the régulateur de l'énergiey commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional environnemental authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income économie ($95.4B PIB) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% PIB). Leader in ecotourism and environnemental policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Lorsque per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Lorsque export tax benefits require environnemental certification, the intérêt chevauchant between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production s'affaiblit.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and environnemental complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): Adopter pesticide reduction targets
Investir dans organic certification and agroforestry transitions
Mettre en œuvre watershed-level environnemental monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): Réformer export incentives to require environnemental certification
MINAE: Renforcer pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative environnemental impact assessments for monoculture expansion
Contactez votre Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa pour exiger pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's environnemental reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Pousser pour green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts environnemental impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for environnemental services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global environnemental leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on environnemental access rights.

CU.s: Command économie ($107.4B PIB, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% PIB, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Lorsque organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, l'intérêt chevauchant dans conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that dépendance du système on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document environnemental degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: Renforcer environnemental monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
Plaider via CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
Pousser pour relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a État à parti unique. Independent société civile, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees environnemental policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island économie ($37.6B PIB) with dominant services sector (76.5% PIB, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership transparence requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership transparence. Lorsque financial secrecy is eliminated, the intérêt chevauchant between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity s'affaiblit.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Partiellement cassable. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's membre de l'UEship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Mettre en œuvre enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
Adopter full beneficial ownership transparence ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: Renforcer beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: Appliquer enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce transparence standards
Contactez votre Βουλευτής in the Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) pour exiger full implementation of EU AML directives
Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced transparence requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
Pousser pour EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an membre de l'UE with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial économie ($347B PIB) with strong manufacturing (19.9% PIB, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). membre de l'UE. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Services à 60.2% PIB.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite exploitation minière is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the intérêt chevauchant between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Lorsque plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the intérêt chevauchant breaks.
Mandatory par centrale émissions and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
Non cassable. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report émissions violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in exploitation minière communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech lanceur d'alerte protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)
ČEZ (majority d'État): Accelerate coal phase-out timeline and énergie renouvelable investment
Sev.en Energy: Développer mine closure and site remediation plans
Investir dans nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Réformer electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Renforcer air quality enforcement in exploitation minière regions
Czech Coal Commission: Mettre en œuvre recommendations on coal phase-out by 2033
Contactez votre Poslanec or Senátor in the Parlament pour exiger a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition measures
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform
Pousser pour Territorial Just Transition Plans with concrete retraining programs
i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for environnemental law, conducting inspections and imposing sanctions. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees environnemental policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% d'État), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite exploitation minière and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent régulateur de l'énergie. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic économie ($424.5B PIB) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Services à 63.5% PIB. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Réformer agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Lorsque the intérêt chevauchant between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates dépendance du système can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)
Document animal welfare violations — report to Fødevarestyrelsen
Use Danish lanceur d'alerte law (Lov om beskyttelse af lanceur d'alertee, 2021)
Danish Crown, Arla: Investir dans plant-based protein alternatives
Mettre en œuvre nitrogen-neutral farming targets across supply chains
Adopter Scope 3 agricultural émissions reporting
Miljøministeriet: Tighten nitrogen quotas for marine ecosystem recovery
Landbrugsstyrelsen: Réformer CAP eco-scheme payments to reward nitrogen-negative farming
Mettre en œuvre the 2021 agreement on green transition of Danish agriculture (55-65% émissions reduction by 2030)
Contactez votre member of the Folketing pour exiger enforcement of agricultural nitrogen reduction targets
Support EU-wide mandatory nitrogen balance disclosure
Advocate for carbon border adjustment on imported animal feed from déforestation regions
Pousser pour Baltic Sea Action Plan implementation through HELCOM
i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for environnemental regulation including agricultural émissions and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — environnemental policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% émissions reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest économie ($124.3B PIB) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and exploitation minière (2.04% PIB — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Services à 59.8% PIB. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Lorsque par mine water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Mandate environnemental bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Lorsque exploitation minière companies pre-fund cleanup, l'intérêt chevauchant dans extraction without environnemental accountability s'affaiblit.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from exploitation minière revenue.
Partiellement cassable. Open-pit exploitation minière disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report environnemental violations to the Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources
Document community health impacts — report to health authorities
Report through Barrick Gold's compliance channels
Barrick Gold (Pueblo Viejo): Mettre en œuvre GISTM tailings management standards
Adopter full water quality disclosure for all operations
Investir dans community development programs per exploitation minière code requirements
Ministry of Energy and Mines: Renforcer environnemental conditions in exploitation minière concessions
Appliquer Mining Law 146-71 environnemental provisions
Publish exploitation minière sector environnemental compliance data
Contactez votre Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional pour exiger mandatory environnemental bonds for exploitation minière operations
Support EITI membership for the Dominican Republic
Plaider via SICA for regional exploitation minière environnemental standards
Pousser pour exploitation minière revenue transparence
i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the exploitation minière directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees exploitation minière policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs exploitation minière concessions and environnemental obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential démocratie.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B PIB) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Agriculture à 14% PIB (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Lorsque production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Lorsque solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, l'intérêt chevauchant dans gas-fired power generation breaks.

Mettre en œuvre par gisement émissions disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes environnemental damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report environnemental violations through Sonatrach internal channels
Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines
Report concerns to international partner compliance systems (Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor)
Sonatrach: Développer solar energy division using Saharan land bank
Mettre en œuvre flaring reduction program
International partners (Eni, TotalEnergies): Apply global émissions standards to Algerian operations
ALNAFT: Renforcer environnemental conditions in exploration and production contracts
CREG: Accelerate domestic énergie renouvelable deployment (the PNER 2030 targets 22GW solar)
ARH: Appliquer flaring reduction regulations
Plaider via OPEC for managed production decline aligned with Paris Agreement
Support Mediterranean Solar Plan and Desertec-style initiatives for Saharan solar export
Pousser pour EITI membership for Algeria
Leverage Algeria's gas supply role to Europe for energy transition dialogue
i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive lanceur d'alerte protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent économie ($124.7B PIB) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Agriculture à 9.5% PIB (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the intérêt chevauchant. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Mettre en œuvre the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Lorsque democratic decisions override extraction interests, the intérêt chevauchant breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block environnemental impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's environnemental data (Amazon déforestation, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
