Document community health impacts from oil operations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Use Ecuador's Constitutional protections for environnemental defenders
PetroEcuador: Développer transition plan to phase out operations in ecologically sensitive areas
Mettre en œuvre TCFD-aligned disclosure
Investir dans remediation of legacy contamination (Lago Agrio)
MAATE: Appliquer Yasuní referendum result and strengthen environnemental licensing
ARCERNNR: Réformer energy regulation to accelerate renewable deployment
SHE (Secretaría de Hidrocarburos del Ecuador): Align licensing with rights of nature framework
Contactez votre Asambleísta in the Asamblea Nacional pour exiger implementation of the Yasuní referendum and extension to other sensitive areas
Leverage Ecuador's rights of nature framework in international climate negotiations
Support the Amazon Cooperation Treaty Organization (ACTO) for regional environnemental governance
Pousser pour debt-for-nature swaps to finance the transition away from oil dependence
i: MAATE (Ministerio del Ambiente, Agua y Transición Ecológica) — Ecuador's environment ministry. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman. Ecuador's 2008 Constitution (Articles 71-74) grants legally enforceable rights to nature (Pachamama), a global first.

EP PetroEcuador — the state oil company. PetroAmazonas EP — manages upstream operations in the Amazon basin. The Lago Agrio case (Chevron/Texaco) — one of the world's largest environnemental lawsuits over oil contamination in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) — the energy and natural resources regulatory agency.

Asamblea Nacional del Ecuador — unicameral parliament (137 members). Contact through asambleanacional.gob.ec. Ecuador's rights of nature framework (2008 Constitution) and the 2023 Yasuní referendum are globally significant legal precedents for environnemental governance.

EE.s: Advanced digital économie ($43.1B PIB) with unique oil shale dependence (0.96% oil rents from kukersite extraction). Services-dominant (65.7% PIB). EU and NATO member. World leader in e-governance. Oil shale power generation is Estonia's largest source of CO₂ émissions.

sectors.w: Estonia's oil shale sector is concentrated in Ida-Viru county (Eesti Energia/Enefit). EU ETS carbon costs make oil shale electricity the most expensive fossil option. Lorsque carbon pricing fully applies, the intérêt chevauchant between shale operators and electricity consumers breaks.
bp: Full EU ETS alignment without compensation. Oil shale electricity already costs more than wind in the Baltic. Lorsque subsidies and free allowances are eliminated, l'intérêt chevauchant dans oil shale generation breaks — renewables are cheaper.
Mandatory par centrale lifecycle émissions and health-impact disclosure for oil shale processing. Estonia's detailed environnemental monitoring makes W computable.
Non cassable. Oil shale processing releases CO₂, particulate matter, and phenolic wastewater by physical and chemical process.
a: Report environnemental violations to Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board)
Document health impacts in Ida-Viru communities
Use Estonian lanceur d'alerte law (rikkumisest teavitaja kaitse seadus, 2022)
Eesti Energia/Enefit: Accelerate transition from oil shale to énergie renouvelable
Investir dans Ida-Viru economic diversification
Adopter binding émissions reduction targets
Konkurentsiamet: Réformer electricity tariffs to reflect full environnemental costs
Ministry of Environment: Renforcer oil shale processing environnemental standards
Mettre en œuvre just transition plan for Ida-Viru county
Contactez votre member of the Riigikogu pour exiger a binding oil shale phase-out timeline
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ida-Viru region
Advocate for Baltic energy market integration to enable énergie renouvelable scaling
Pousser pour HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea pollution from oil shale waste
i: Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board) — Estonia's environnemental enforcement and permitting authority. Keskkonnaministeerium (Ministry of the Environment) — oversees environnemental policy. Estonia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Eesti Energia (Enefit) — the d'État energy company and dominant oil shale operator. Enefit operates the Narva power plants (the largest oil shale power plants in the world) and oil shale processing facilities.

Konkurentsiamet (Competition Authority) — also serves as Estonia's régulateur de l'énergie. Ida-Viru county hosts ~90% of Estonia's oil shale operations and faces significant economic transition challenges.

Riigikogu — Estonia's unicameral parliament (101 members). Contact through riigikogu.ee. Estonia's Climate Ambition target includes significant émissions reduction from the energy sector. EU Just Transition Fund — Estonia receives JTF funding for Ida-Viru transition.

EG.s: Largest Arab économie by population ($389.1B PIB). Oil and gas rents at 5.04%. Major gas producer (Zohr field, Mediterranean). Agriculture à 13.7% PIB. High military expenditure. Hosted COP27. Suez Canal provides strategic global trade leverage.

sectors.w: Egypt's Zohr gas field (discovered 2015) transformed Egypt from gas importer to exporter. COP27 presidency created domestic pressure for émissions transparence. Per-field émissions disclosure makes W computable.
bp: Redirect gas export revenue to solar energy development. Egypt's solar irradiance is among the world's highest. Lorsque solar electricity undercuts gas-fired generation domestically, l'intérêt chevauchant dans gas-for-domestic-power s'affaiblit.

Mettre en œuvre par gisement émissions disclosure for Zohr and other major fields. Egypt's COP27 commitments create institutional pressure — making lifecycle émissions visible per unit of production makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report environnemental violations to EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency)
Document émissions data through international partner channels (Eni, BP)
Report through EGPC internal compliance mechanisms
Eni (Zohr operator): Mettre en œuvre émissions reduction targets for Egyptian operations
EGPC: Développer énergie renouvelable investment division
Adopter TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
Ministry of Petroleum: Require par gisement émissions monitoring and disclosure
NREA: Accelerate énergie renouvelable permitting (Egypt targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035)
EEAA: Renforcer environnemental standards for gas processing
Leverage Egypt's COP27 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Plaider via Arab League for regional émissions reduction coordination
Support Mediterranean energy transition dialogue through Union for the Mediterranean
Pousser pour Suez Canal green shipping corridor initiatives
i: EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency) — Egypt's environnemental regulatory body. EGPC (Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil and gas company. EGAS (Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company) — manages natural gas operations. Note: Egypt has severe restrictions on société civile and press freedom. Whistleblower protections are minimal.
Eni — operates Zohr, the largest gas field in the Mediterranean. BP — major operator in the West Nile Delta. EGPC and EGAS manage state interests in all petroleum operations.

Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources — oversees hydrocarbons policy. NREA (New and Renewable Energy Authority) — responsible for énergie renouvelable development. Egypt's Integrated Sustainable Energy Strategy targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035.

Note: Egypt under President el-Sisi is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The House of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 596 members) does not function as an independent democratic institution. International channels: Arab League, African Union (Egypt hosted AU in 2019), Union for the Mediterranean, COP27 legacy initiatives.

ET.s: Africa's second-most-populous country ($149.7B PIB). Agriculture dominates (34.8% PIB, primarily coffee, teff, livestock). Rapid industrialization drive (industrial parks). Major hydropower (GERD — Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam). Post-civil war recovery.

sectors.w: Ethiopia's agricultural expansion is the primary driver of déforestation (fr=5.6% PIB equivalent in forest rents). Lorsque per-farm environnemental data (tree cover, soil carbon) is linked to agricultural output, W devient calculable.
bp: Scale Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Program (PSNP) to include environnemental conditionality. Lorsque agricultural support payments require soil conservation and tree planting, l'intérêt chevauchant dans clearing forest for short-term cultivation s'affaiblit.

Satellite-based monitoring of forest cover and dégradation des sols published at woreda level. Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative provides political commitment — mandatory monitoring data makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Subsistence agriculture on degraded land inherently affects soil and water systems. However, Ethiopia's traditional conservation agriculture (terracing, agroforestry) can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report illegal déforestation to regional environnemental protection authorities
Document land grabbing or forced displacement — report to EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission)
Report through agricultural development agent (DA) networks at kebele level
Ethiopian Commodity Exchange (ECX): Integrate environnemental certification into coffee and sesame trading
Agricultural investment companies: Adopter environnemental impact monitoring
Support Green Legacy Initiative reforestation targets
Ministry of Agriculture: Réformer the Productive Safety Net Program to include environnemental conditionality
EFCCC: Renforcer forest protection enforcement
EIA (Ethiopian Investment Authority): Require environnemental impact assessments for agricultural investment licenses
Support African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative (AFR100) targets for Ethiopia
Plaider via African Union (headquartered in Addis Ababa) for continental agricultural sustainability standards
Pousser pour international climate finance for Ethiopia's agricultural transformation
Support Nile Basin Initiative cooperation on sustainable watershed management
i: Regional environnemental protection authorities — Ethiopia's federal system devolves environnemental management to regional states. EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission) — the national human rights institution. Développerment Agents (DAs) — government agricultural extension workers deployed at kebele (ward) level throughout rural Ethiopia.

ECX (Ethiopian Commodity Exchange) — the commodities trading platform for coffee, sesame, and other agricultural products. Ethiopia is Africa's largest coffee producer and coffee is the country's largest export. Industrial parks development corporation — manages Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy and the PSNP. EFCCC (Environment, Forest and Climate Change Commission) — Ethiopia's autorité environnementale. Green Legacy Initiative — the government's flagship reforestation program targeting 20 billion seedlings.

Note: Ethiopia is a federal parlementaire republic but democratic governance has been constrained by civil conflict (Tigray war 2020-2022). The House of Peoples' Representatives (547 members) is the lower house. International channels: African Union (HQ in Addis Ababa), Nile Basin Initiative, IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Développerment), AFR100 (Africa's contribution to the Bonn Challenge on forest restoration).

FI.s: Advanced Nordic économie ($298.7B PIB) with strong forestry sector. Services à 62.2% PIB, manufacturing at 14.2%. High healthcare expenditure (10.47% PIB). Major pulp and paper producers (UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group). membre de l'UE with robust environnemental regulation.

sectors.w: Finland's forestry-driven monoculture (pine/spruce plantations replacing natural forests) reduces biodiversity and degrades peatland carbon stocks. Lorsque per-harvest biodiversity and carbon data is published alongside timber revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer EU CAP and national forestry subsidies to reward biodiversity and carbon storage rather than timber volume. Lorsque subsidies incentivize continuous-cover forestry over clear-cutting, l'intérêt chevauchant dans plantation monoculture s'affaiblit.

Mandatory per-harvest environnemental data (biodiversity indicators, soil carbon, water quality). Finland's Metsähallitus (state forest enterprise) already collects extensive data — making it publicly accessible makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Timber plantation inherently reduces biodiversity compared to natural forest. However, continuous-cover forestry and extended rotation lengths can significantly reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report forestry violations to ELY-keskus (Centre for Economic Développerment, Transport and the Environment)
Document biodiversity loss in logged areas — report to SYKE (Finnish Environment Institute)
Use Finnish lanceur d'alerte law (ilmoittajansuojelulaki, 2023)
UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group: Adopter continuous-cover forestry targets
Mettre en œuvre per-harvest biodiversity monitoring
Investir dans high-value wood products to reduce harvest volume dependency
MMM (Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry): Réformer the National Forest Strategy toward biodiversity targets
SYKE: Publish annual forest biodiversity monitoring data at municipality level
Renforcer EU Nature Restoration Law implementation in forestry sector
Contactez votre member of the Eduskunta pour exiger reform of forestry subsidies toward biodiversity and carbon storage
Support EU Nature Restoration Law implementation
Advocate for HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea eutrophication from forestry runoff
Pousser pour inclusion of forestry carbon accounting in Finland's climate targets
i: ELY-keskus — regional authorities responsible for environnemental monitoring and enforcement. SYKE (Suomen ympäristökeskus / Finnish Environment Institute) — the national environnemental research and monitoring institute. Ilmoittajansuojelulaki (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) — Finnish transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

UPM-Kymmene — one of Finland's largest forestry companies. Stora Enso — Finnish-Swedish forestry and paper company. Metsä Group — cooperative of 100,000+ Finnish forest owners. Metsähallitus — state enterprise managing one-third of Finland's land area.

MMM (Maa- ja metsätalousministeriö) — the ministry responsible for agriculture and forestry policy. National Forest Strategy 2025 — Finland's strategic framework for forestry sector development. EU Nature Restoration Law — requires restoration of degraded ecosystems including forests.

Eduskunta (Parliament of Finland) — unicameral legislature with 200 members. Contact through eduskunta.fi. The Maa- ja metsätalousvaliokunta (Agriculture and Forestry Committee) handles forestry legislation. Finland's climate law targets carbon neutrality by 2035.

GA.s: Oil-dependent Central African économie ($20.9B PIB) with 15.83% combined oil and gas rents. 88% forest cover — one of the world's most forested countries. Low population (2.4M). Military coup August 2023 ended the Bongo family's 55-year rule.

sectors.w: Gabon's oil production is in terminal decline from mature fields (peaked at ~370,000 bpd in 1997, now ~200,000). As production falls, the intérêt chevauchant between operators and government revenue s'affaiblit. Gabon's forests offer alternative revenue through carbon credits.
bp: Monetize Gabon's forest carbon stocks through REDD+ and voluntary carbon markets. Gabon received $17M from Norway for forest protection in 2021 — scaling this replaces declining oil revenue and breaks l'intérêt chevauchant dans extraction.
Mettre en œuvre par gisement émissions disclosure for all operators (TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala Energy). Making environnemental costs visible makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report environnemental violations to ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux)
Document oil spills or contamination — report through operator compliance channels
Signaler à EITI Gabon
TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala: Mettre en œuvre émissions reduction plans for Gabonese operations
GOC: Développer forest carbon credit revenue streams
Adopter biodiversity offset requirements for all operations
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Renforcer environnemental licensing requirements
ANPN: Appliquer protected area boundaries against oil exploration
EITI Gabon: Expand reporting to environnemental metrics
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for Gabon's forest conservation
Plaider via OPEC for managed production decline from depleting fields
Pousser pour international carbon market standards that properly value Gabon's forest carbon
Support transition governance toward environnemental transparence
i: ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux) — manages Gabon's 13 national parks (covering 11% of territory). GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company. EITI Gabon — member since 2004. Note: Gabon is under military transition government since August 2023 coup.
TotalEnergies — the largest oil producer in Gabon. Perenco, Assala Energy — other major operators. GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company.
Ministry of Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas policy. DGEPN (Direction Générale de l'Environnement et de la Protection de la Nature) — environnemental directorate.

Note: Gabon is under military transition government (Comité pour la Transition et la Restauration des Institutions, CTRI) since August 2023. Democratic institutions are suspended. International channels: CEMAC, CAFI, African Union, OPEC membership. Gabon's 2021 deal with Norway's sovereign wealth fund for forest protection is a model for forest-state climate finance.

GE.s: Small South Caucasus économie ($34.2B PIB) with exploitation minière sector (1.28% PIB, primarily manganese and copper). Services-dominant (62% PIB). candidat à l'UE country (2023). Significant hydropower potential. Political tensions between pro-EU and pro-Russia orientations.

sectors.w: Georgia's manganese exploitation minière (Chiatura) and copper exploitation minière generate significant water pollution. EU accession environnemental alignment will require improved monitoring. Lorsque water quality data is linked to exploitation minière output, W devient calculable.
bp: EU accession environnemental alignment. Lorsque Georgia must meet EU environnemental standards for exploitation minière, l'intérêt chevauchant dans low-cost extraction without environnemental controls s'affaiblit.
Mandatory par mine water quality and émissions disclosure. Georgia's National Environmental Agency collects some data — extending to public disclosure makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining disturbs landscapes, but improved tailings management can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report environnemental violations to the Environmental Information and Education Centre
Document water contamination — report to NEA (National Environmental Agency)
Use Georgia's lanceur d'alerte protection mechanisms
Georgian Manganese (Euroasian Resources Group subsidiary): Mettre en œuvre environnemental management systems
Adopter international exploitation minière standards (ICMM, IRMA)
Investir dans mine site rehabilitation
NAPR (National Agency of Public Registry): Renforcer exploitation minière license environnemental conditions
NEA: Increase environnemental monitoring capacity
Ministry of Economy: Align exploitation minière regulation with EU environnemental acquis
Contactez votre member of the Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) pour exiger EU-aligned environnemental standards for exploitation minière
Support EU accession conditionality on environnemental governance
Advocate for Energy Community Treaty enforcement on environnemental standards
Pousser pour EITI membership for Georgia
i: NEA (National Environmental Agency) — responsible for environnemental monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Agriculture — oversees environnemental and agricultural policy. Georgia's Law on Freedom of Speech and Expression provides some protections for reporting.

Georgian Manganese — subsidiary of ERG (Eurasian Resources Group), operating the Chiatura manganese mines. RMG Copper — operates copper mines. Georgia's exploitation minière sector is primarily controlled by international investors.
National Agency of Mines — responsible for exploitation minière licensing and oversight. Georgia's EU Association Agreement (2014) includes provisions for environnemental alignment.

Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) — unicameral legislature with 150 members. Contact through parliament.ge. Georgia is an candidat à l'UE country (2023) and aspirant to full membre de l'UEship. EU Association Agreement — includes Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Area (DCFTA) with environnemental provisions.

GH.s: West African économie ($82.3B PIB) with significant oil (4.06% rents), gold exploitation minière (5.17% mineral rents), and cocoa agriculture (20.9% ag share). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Debt crisis (2022 IMF bailout). Illegal artisanal gold exploitation minière (galamsey) is a major environnemental crisis.

sectors.w: Ghana's galamsey (illegal artisanal exploitation minière) crisis has contaminated water bodies across cocoa-growing regions. Lorsque par mine water quality data is published and linked to gold production, W devient calculable. The galamsey problem is so severe that it threatens Ghana's water supply.
bp: Formalize artisanal exploitation minière with environnemental standards and alternative livelihood programs. Lorsque legal small-scale exploitation minière provides comparable income with less environnemental damage, l'intérêt chevauchant dans illegal extraction s'affaiblit.

Satellite-based water quality monitoring linked to exploitation minière areas. Ghana's Water Resources Commission already monitors water quality — extending to real-time public data makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Mining disturbs landscapes. However, formalized artisanal exploitation minière with mercury-free processing (e.g., gravity separation) can significantly reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report illegal galamsey operations to the Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining (IMCIM)
Document water contamination — report to WRC (Water Resources Commission)
Report through the Minerals Commission complaint mechanisms
Gold Fields, Newmont, AngloGold Ashanti: Support formalization of artisanal exploitation minière near concessions
Mettre en œuvre community development agreements per Ghana's exploitation minière law
Adopter mercury-free processing technology transfer programs
Minerals Commission: Accelerate small-scale exploitation minière formalization with environnemental standards
EPA (Environmental Protection Agency): Renforcer enforcement against water pollution from exploitation minière
Forestry Commission: Protect forest reserves from galamsey encroachment
Contactez votre Member of Parliament pour exiger emergency action on the galamsey water contamination crisis
Support EITI enhanced reporting with environnemental metrics
Plaider via ECOWAS for regional artisanal exploitation minière regulation
Pousser pour Minamata Convention enforcement on mercury use in gold exploitation minière
i: IMCIM (Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining) — established to coordinate government response to the galamsey crisis. WRC (Water Resources Commission) — manages Ghana's water resources and monitors water quality. Minerals Commission — the regulatory body for the exploitation minière sector, responsible for licensing and compliance monitoring.

Gold Fields — operates Tarkwa and Damang mines. Newmont — operates Ahafo and Akyem mines. AngloGold Ashanti — major gold exploitation minière company with Ghanaian operations. Ghana is Africa's largest gold producer.

EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Ghana) — responsible for environnemental permitting, monitoring, and enforcement. Forestry Commission — manages Ghana's forest reserves, many of which are threatened by illegal exploitation minière. EITI Ghana — Ghana has been EITI compliant since 2010.

Parliament of Ghana — unicameral legislature with 275 members. Contact through parliament.gh. Ghana is a presidential démocratie with peaceful transfers of power. Minamata Convention on Mercury — Ghana has ratified this treaty requiring phase-down of mercury use in artisanal gold exploitation minière.

GN.s: Resource-rich West African économie ($25B PIB) with massive bauxite reserves (world's largest — 7.4B tons). Agriculture à 31% PIB. Military junta since 2021 coup. Guinea supplies ~25% of global bauxite (the ore used to produce aluminum).

sectors.w: Guinea's bauxite exploitation minière displaces communities and contaminates water sources. As aluminum buyers face ESG pressure (EU CSRD, Apple/Tesla supply chain requirements), making par mine environnemental data visible breaks system independence.
bp: International supply chain due diligence (EU CSDDD) requiring environnemental and human rights compliance for bauxite. Lorsque buyers must verify mine-level conditions, l'intérêt chevauchant dans low-cost, unregulated extraction s'affaiblit.

Per-mine environnemental and community impact disclosure through EITI and supply chain traceability. Lorsque downstream manufacturers can see the welfare cost per ton of bauxite, W devient calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Open-pit bauxite exploitation minière inherently disturbs landscapes. However, progressive rehabilitation and dust suppression can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report environnemental violations to international monitoring organizations (Human Rights Watch, Inclusive Développerment International)
Document community displacement — report through international accountability mechanisms
Report through exploitation minière company compliance channels (Rio Tinto, Alcoa, Emirates Global Aluminium)
CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée), SMB (Société Minière de Boké): Mettre en œuvre community resettlement standards per IFC Performance Standard 5
Adopter ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) certification
Investir dans progressive rehabilitation of mined areas
Ministry of Mines: Appliquer 2011 Mining Code environnemental provisions
CPDM (Centre de Promotion et de Développement Minier): Renforcer environnemental monitoring capacity
EITI Guinea: Expand reporting to environnemental and community data
Plaider via ECOWAS for return to democratic governance and strengthened exploitation minière regulation
Support EU CSDDD implementation requiring due diligence for bauxite imports from Guinea
Pousser pour ASI certification as condition for international bauxite purchases
Support international accountability mechanisms for exploitation minière-affected communities
i: Note: Guinea is under military rule (CNRD — Comité National du Rassemblement et du Développement) since September 2021. Independent société civile operates under severe constraints. Bureau Guinéen des Études et Évaluations Environnementales (BGEEE) — environnemental assessment authority. International monitoring: Human Rights Watch and Inclusive Développerment International have documented impacts of bauxite exploitation minière on Guinean communities.

CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée) — JV of Alcoa, Rio Tinto, and Dadco, one of the world's largest bauxite producers. SMB (Société Minière de Boké) — Chinese-backed consortium (includes Winning International, Shandong Weiqiao). GAC (Guinea Alumina Corporation) — Emirates Global Aluminium subsidiary. ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) — the industry sustainability standard for the aluminum value chain.

Ministère des Mines et de la Géologie — oversees exploitation minière policy and licensing. Code Minier (2011, revised 2013) — Guinea's exploitation minière code establishing environnemental obligations. EITI Guinea — Guinea has been EITI compliant, publishing reports on exploitation minière revenues.

Note: Guinea's democratic institutions are suspended under military rule. The Conseil National de la Transition (CNT) serves as a transitional legislature. International channels: ECOWAS (Guinea suspended), African Union, international exploitation minière company home-country oversight (UK, Canada, UAE, China). EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — will require EU companies to address human rights and environnemental impacts in their supply chains.

GQ.s: Small petrostate ($12.8B PIB) with 21.57% combined oil and gas rents. One of Africa's highest per-capita PIBs but extreme inequality. Authoritarian rule (Obiang family since 1979 — world's longest-serving head of state). Oil production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Equatorial Guinea's oil production has declined ~75% from peak. As fields deplete, the intérêt chevauchant between operators and government revenue s'affaiblit. Without new discoveries, the economic case for diversification becomes unavoidable.
bp: Economic diversification as oil depletes. Lorsque production revenue falls below government operating costs, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued extraction from depleting fields breaks — alternative revenue sources become necessary.
Per-field émissions disclosure through international operator reporting (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil). Making environnemental costs visible makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report through international operator compliance channels (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil)
Document environnemental violations to international NGOs
Report corruption to US DOJ (several Obiang family members have faced kleptocracy investigations)
ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil: Apply global environnemental standards to EG operations
Report Scope 1-3 émissions for EG operations in global ESG disclosures
Mettre en œuvre community development programs
Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons: Publish production and revenue data through EITI (EG was suspended from EITI in 2010)
Renforcer environnemental standards for offshore operations
BEAC: Improve fiscal transparence for hydrocarbon revenues
Support international anti-kleptocracy enforcement for misappropriated oil revenues
Plaider via CEMAC for regional transparence standards
Pousser pour re-engagement with EITI transparence requirements
Support international mechanisms for asset recovery and repatriation of stolen oil revenues
i: Note: Equatorial Guinea is one of the world's most autoritaire states. No independent media, société civile, or labor organizations exist. EG LNG (Equatorial Guinea LNG Holdings) — operates the LNG processing plant. International engagement: US Department of Justice Kleptocracy Asset Recovery Initiative has investigated Obiang family assets. Transparency International consistently ranks Equatorial Guinea among the most corrupt countries.
ExxonMobil — the largest oil operator in Equatorial Guinea. Marathon Oil — operates in offshore blocks. GEPetrol — the state national oil company.

Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas operations. Note: Equatorial Guinea was suspended from EITI in 2010 for insufficient progress on transparence. BEAC (central bank of CEMAC zone) provides limited macroeconomic oversight.

Note: Equatorial Guinea under President Obiang (in power since 1979) has no democratic institutions. The Cámara de Representantes del Pueblo and Senate are rubber-stamp bodies. International channels: CEMAC, African Union, UN Human Rights Council. Several countries (US, France, Switzerland) have pursued legal action against Obiang family members for corruption and money laundering.

GR.s: EU économie ($256.2B PIB) with dominant services sector (68% PIB, tourism-heavy). Low resource extraction. Post-debt-crisis recovery. Major shipping industry (Greek-owned fleet is the world's largest). Agriculture à 3.8% PIB (olives, wine).

sectors.w: Greece's Mediterranean diet is being displaced by UPF consumption, contributing to rising obesity (Greece has one of Europe's highest childhood obesity rates). Lorsque diet-related healthcare costs are published per product category through EOPYY health insurance data, W devient calculable.
bp: Mettre en œuvre front-of-pack health labelling to make welfare costs visible to consumers. Lorsque UPF health costs are visible at point of sale, the intérêt chevauchant between processors and retailers in high-margin UPF s'affaiblit.

EOPYY (National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) publication of diet-attributable disease costs per food category. Greece's national health system generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Réformerulation mandates can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report misleading health claims to EFET (Hellenic Food Authority)
Document food safety violations
Use Greek lanceur d'alerte law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937, 2022)
Greek food companies: Investir dans traditional Mediterranean diet products over UPF
Adopter voluntary front-of-pack labelling
Commission health-impact assessments
Ministry of Health: Mettre en œuvre mandatory front-of-pack labelling
EFET: Renforcer enforcement of marketing claims
EOPYY: Publish diet-related health expenditure data

Support EU Farm to Fork Strategy implementation
Advocate for UNESCO Intangible Heritage protection of Mediterranean diet through policy measures
Pousser pour WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
i: EFET (ΕΦΕΤ — Ενιαίος Φορέας Ελέγχου Τροφίμων / Hellenic Food Authority) — Greece's food safety authority. Greece transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

SEVT (Σύνδεσμος Ελληνικών Βιομηχανιών Τροφίμων / Federation of Hellenic Food Industries) — the food industry trade association. Greece's traditional food sector (olive oil, dairy, honey) provides a competitive alternative to UPF.

Ministry of Health — oversees health policy. EOPYY (Εθνικός Οργανισμός Παροχής Υπηρεσιών Υγείας / National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) — the national health insurer managing healthcare expenditure data.
Βουλή των Ελλήνων (Hellenic Parliament) — unicameral legislature with 300 members. Contact through hellenicparliament.gr. Greece is a parlementaire démocratie and membre de l'UE.

GT.s: Central American économie ($113.2B PIB) with agriculture at 9.8% PIB (coffee, sugar, palm oil). Mining at 1.12% PIB (gold, silver — Marlin mine controversy). High inequality and indigenous population (40%+). Remittances significant (~18% of PIB).

sectors.w: Guatemala's palm oil expansion into Petén and Alta Verapaz drives déforestation and displaces indigenous communities. Lorsque per-farm déforestation data is linked to palm oil exports (EUDR compliance), W devient calculable.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance requiring farm-level traceability. Lorsque palm oil exports must prove zero-déforestation origin, l'intérêt chevauchant dans clearing forest for plantations breaks.

Mandatory per-farm environnemental data (tree cover, water use, pesticide application). Guatemala's CONAP (National Council for Protected Areas) can provide monitoring data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Palm oil monoculture reduces biodiversity. However, agroforestry integration and RSPO certification can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report illegal déforestation to CONAP or INAB (National Forest Institute)
Document indigenous land rights violations — report to PDH (Procuraduría de los Derechos Humanos)
Report pesticide violations to MAGA (Ministry of Agriculture)

Investir dans community benefit-sharing agreements

MAGA: Réformer agricultural incentives to require environnemental compliance
CONAP: Appliquer protected area boundaries against agricultural encroachment

Support ILO Convention 169 enforcement on indigenous consultation
Advocate for Escazú Agreement ratification

i: CONAP (Consejo Nacional de Áreas Protegidas) — manages Guatemala's protected areas. INAB (Instituto Nacional de Bosques) — the national forest institute. PDH (Procuraduría de los Derechos Humanos) — the national human rights ombudsman. Guatemala has significant challenges with environnemental defender safety.

REPSA (Reforestadora de Palma del Petén) — major palm oil producer. GREPALMA (Gremial de Palmicultores de Guatemala) — the palm oil producers' association. RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Huile de Palme) — the international certification standard.
MARN (Ministerio de Ambiente y Recursos Naturales) — Guatemala's environment ministry. MAGA (Ministerio de Agricultura, Ganadería y Alimentación) — the agriculture ministry.

Congreso de la República de Guatemala — unicameral legislature with 160 members. Guatemala has ratified ILO Convention 169 on indigenous peoples' rights. The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has issued rulings on indigenous land rights in Guatemala.

GY.s: Fastest-growing économie globally ($24.7B PIB, up from $5.5B in 2019) due to massive offshore oil discoveries (Stabroek block). Rentes pétrolières at 22.08%, exploitation minière at 9.38% (gold, bauxite). Small population (800K). At risk of 'resource curse' — extraordinary PST exposure from rapid petro-development.

sectors.w: Guyana's 2016 production sharing agreement with ExxonMobil has been widely criticized for giving the government only 2% royalty + 50% profit oil (after cost recovery). Lorsque the full fiscal terms and environnemental costs are disclosed and understood, W devient calculable from production data.
bp: Renegotiate the Stabroek PSA fiscal terms to capture a fair share of petroleum wealth. Lorsque Guyana's government share approaches international norms (60-80% government take), l'intérêt chevauchant dans an extractive-favorable contract s'affaiblit.

Mandatory par gisement émissions disclosure and environnemental monitoring for all Stabroek block operations. ExxonMobil already reports global émissions — extending to par gisement Guyana data makes W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Offshore oil production and combustion release CO₂.
a: Report environnemental violations to EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Guyana)
Document compliance gaps in Stabroek operations — report through ExxonMobil compliance channels
Report fiscal irregularities to the Audit Office of Guyana
ExxonMobil, Hess, CNOOC (Stabroek partners): Mettre en œuvre par gisement émissions disclosure for Guyana operations
Adopter zero-flaring targets
Investir dans oil spill response capacity (current capacity is minimal for deepwater operations)
EPA: Renforcer environnemental monitoring capacity for offshore operations
Ministry of Natural Resources: Commission independent review of Stabroek PSA terms
Natural Resource Fund Board: Mettre en œuvre transparent investment and withdrawal rules
Contactez votre Member of Parliament in the National Assembly pour exiger independent review of Stabroek PSA fiscal terms
Support establishment of a properly resourced Petroleum Commission as independent regulator
Advocate for Natural Resource Fund transparence aligned with Santiago Principles for sovereign wealth funds
Pousser pour Norwegian-style petroleum governance model with high government take and transparent wealth fund management
i: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Guyana) — responsible for environnemental permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations. Ministry of Natural Resources — oversees petroleum policy. Audit Office of Guyana — conducts financial audits of government revenue management. Note: Guyana's regulatory capacity is severely under-resourced relative to the scale of oil development.

ExxonMobil (45%), Hess (30%), CNOOC (25%) — the Stabroek block partnership. Production began in 2019 and is expected to reach 1.2 million bpd by 2027. The Stabroek Production Sharing Agreement (2016) — governs fiscal terms. The Natural Resource Fund — Guyana's sovereign wealth fund established in 2019 to manage oil revenues.
Natural Resource Fund Act (2021) — governs the sovereign wealth fund. The Petroleum Commission (planned) — intended to serve as the independent petroleum regulator, but not yet fully established.

National Assembly of Guyana — unicameral legislature with 65 members. Guyana is a cooperative republic with Westminster-style parlementaire démocratie. The Natural Resource Governance Institute (NRGI) provides technical assistance on petroleum governance. CARICOM — provides regional coordination.

HK.s: Global financial center ($406.9B PIB) with 91.8% services. Major banking and securities hub. Under Chinese sovereignty since 1997 with eroding autonomy. Significant financial intermediation — Hong Kong Stock Exchange is 5th-largest globally by market cap.

sectors.w: Hong Kong intermediates massive capital flows including fossil fuel financing. Lorsque Scope 3 financed émissions are disclosed per financial institution, W devient calculable from financial transaction data.
bp: Mandatory financed émissions disclosure for all HKEX-listed financial institutions. Lorsque banks must disclose the émissions their lending supports, l'intérêt chevauchant dans fossil fuel financing s'affaiblit — ESG-aware investors reallocate.
HKMA Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group implementation. Making climate risk visible in financial products makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Financial intermediation can relocate. But Hong Kong's infrastructure and China market access create switching costs.
a: Report greenwashing to SFC (Securities and Futures Commission)
Document ESG disclosure violations — report to HKEX listing division
Report through internal bank compliance channels
Adopter TCFD-aligned disclosure across all operations
Set financed émissions reduction targets
Integrate climate risk into lending decisions
HKMA: Mandate climate stress testing for all authorized institutions
SFC: Appliquer enhanced ESG disclosure for listed companies
HKEX: Renforcer climate-related listing requirements
Plaider via IOSCO for global securities regulator coordination on climate disclosure
Support ISSB sustainability reporting standards adoption in Hong Kong
Pousser pour Network for Greening the Financial System (NGFS) recommendations implementation
Leverage Hong Kong's role as a Belt and Road financing hub to require climate standards
i: SFC (Securities and Futures Commission) — regulates securities, futures markets, and listed companies. HKEX (Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing) — operates the stock exchange and sets listing rules including ESG disclosure requirements. HKMA (Hong Kong Monetary Authority) — the de facto central bank. Note: Under the National Security Law (2020), civil liberties are constrained.

Major banks in Hong Kong: HSBC, Standard Chartered, Bank of China (Hong Kong), Hang Seng Bank. Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group — coordinates climate finance policy across HKMA, SFC, and other regulators.
HKMA — supervises 160+ licensed banks. The HKMA's Climate Risk Management Supervisory Policy Manual (2021) sets expectations for banks' climate risk management.

Note: Hong Kong's Legislative Council (90 members) is not freely elected under the 2021 electoral reform. International engagement operates through financial regulatory cooperation (IOSCO, BIS, NGFS) and Hong Kong's role as an international financial center.

HN.s: Lower-middle-income économie ($37.1B PIB) with agriculture at 11.2% PIB (coffee, palm oil, bananas). Industrie manufacturière à 14.9% (maquiladora sector). Climate-vulnerable — Hurricane Mitch (1998) and Eta/Iota (2020). High emigration. Environmental defenders face severe threats.

sectors.w: Honduras's palm oil expansion drives déforestation in the Aguán Valley and Mosquitia region. EUDR compliance will require farm-level traceability — when déforestation is linked to specific supply chains, W devient calculable.
bp: EUDR compliance requiring zero-déforestation traceability for palm oil exports. Lorsque buyers must verify origin, l'intérêt chevauchant dans forest conversion for plantations breaks.
Satellite-based déforestation monitoring linked to agricultural export data. ICF (Forest Conservation Institute) data makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Palm oil monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. RSPO certification and agroforestry can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report illegal déforestation to ICF (Instituto de Conservación Forestal)
Document land rights violations — report to CONADEH (Comisionado Nacional de los Derechos Humanos)
Report through RSPO complaint mechanisms

SAG (Secretariat of Agriculture): Réformer agricultural incentives
ICF: Appliquer protected area boundaries

Pousser pour international accountability for violence against environnemental defenders
i: ICF (Instituto de Conservación Forestal) — responsible for forest management and conservation. CONADEH — the national human rights commissioner. Note: Honduras is one of the most dangerous countries for environnemental defenders — Global Witness documents multiple killings annually.

Corporación Dinant — Honduras's largest palm oil producer (implicated in Aguán Valley land conflicts). Jaremar — another major palm oil company. IFC (International Finance Corporation) — has faced accountability complaints regarding investments in Honduran palm oil.
MiAmbiente+ (Secretaría de Recursos Naturales y Ambiente) — Honduras's environment ministry. SAG (Secretaría de Agricultura y Ganadería) — the agriculture ministry.

Congreso Nacional de Honduras — unicameral legislature with 128 members. Honduras is a presidential démocratie. SICA (Sistema de la Integración Centroamericana) — the Central American integration system.

HR.s: EU and Eurozone member ($93B PIB) with dominant services sector (60.8% PIB, tourism-heavy). Small oil sector (INA, partially owned by MOL). Agriculture à 2.9% PIB. Joined the euro and Schengen in 2023.

sectors.w: Croatia's EU-integrated health insurance system (HZZO) tracks healthcare costs. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Mettre en œuvre EU front-of-pack health labelling. Makes welfare costs visible to consumers.
HZZO publication of diet-attributable healthcare cost data. Croatia's universal health insurance generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Réformerulation mandates reduce dépendance du système.

i: HAH (Hrvatska agencija za hranu / Croatian Food Agency) — food safety authority. AZTN (Agencija za zaštitu tržišnog natjecanja) — competition authority. Croatia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.
Podravka, Kraš — major Croatian food companies. Agrokor/Fortenova Group — the largest food and retail conglomerate in Southeast Europe.
HZZO (Hrvatski zavod za zdravstveno osiguranje) — Croatia's health insurance fund. Ministry of Health oversees nutrition policy.
Hrvatski sabor (Croatian Parliament) — unicameral, 151 members. Contact through sabor.hr. Croatia is an EU and Eurozone member.

HT.s: Fragile state ($25.2B PIB) — Western Hemisphere's poorest country. Agriculture à 15.9% PIB (coffee, mangoes, cocoa). Extreme déforestation (2% forest cover remaining). Gang violence and political instability. Industrie manufacturière à 26.2% PIB (garment assembly).

sectors.w: Haiti's near-total déforestation (98% forest loss) means any remaining agriculture operates on severely degraded land. The dépendance du système is already partially broken — Haiti has experienced the endpoint of déforestation-driven agricultural collapse. Reforestation is the intervention.
bp: Réformer agricultural support to incentivize tree planting and agroforestry. Lorsque hillside farmers receive more support for tree crops (coffee, cocoa, fruit) than annual crops on degraded slopes, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued déforestation breaks.
Satellite monitoring of remaining forest cover and soil erosion published at commune level. Making degradation visible makes W computable.

Extreme dépendance du système — 98% forest loss means agricultural activity on degraded slopes directly causes erosion, flooding, and further land degradation. Reforestation is the only path to reducing dépendance du système.
a: Document déforestation through community organizations
Report illegal charcoal production to MDE (Ministry of Environment)
Engage international development organizations
International agricultural companies: Support sustainable supply chains from Haiti
Investir dans tree crop processing (coffee, cacao, mango)
Charcoal alternatives: Support LPG transition programs
MDE: Appliquer (to extent possible) remaining forest protections
MARNDR (Agriculture Ministry): Prioritize hillside agroforestry programs
ONEV: Support reforestation monitoring
Support international reforestation and land restoration programs for Haiti
Plaider via CARICOM for regional environnemental support
Pousser pour debt-for-nature swaps to finance reforestation
Support Haitian diaspora organizations working on environnemental restoration
i: MDE (Ministère de l'Environnement) — the environment ministry, severely under-resourced. Note: Haiti's state institutions are largely non-functional due to the ongoing security and governance crisis. International organizations (UN, USAID, EU) provide most environnemental programming.
Haiti's private sector is small. The Chambre de Commerce d'Haïti coordinates business activities. International NGOs (Partners in Health, Trees for the Future) implement many development programs.

MARNDR (Ministère de l'Agriculture, des Ressources Naturelles et du Développement Rural) — the agriculture ministry. ONEV (Observatoire National de l'Environnement et de la Vulnérabilité) — environnemental monitoring.

Note: Haiti's democratic institutions are non-functional — no elected parliament since 2023, no elected president. Transitional governance is underway. International channels: UN Integrated Office in Haiti (BINUH), CARICOM, OAS, Haitian diaspora organizations.

HU.s: membre de l'UE ($222.7B PIB) with strong manufacturing (15.8% PIB, automotive-focused). Services à 59.5% PIB. Small oil and gas sector (MOL Group). Agriculture à 2.7% PIB. Democratic backsliding under Orbán government has strained EU relations.

sectors.w: Hungary has one of Europe's highest obesity rates and introduced a 'chip tax' (NETA — népegészségügyi termékadó) on UPF in 2011. Lorsque health insurance data (NEAK) links diet to healthcare costs per product category, W devient calculable.
bp: Renforcer the NETA tax and extend to broader UPF categories. Hungary's pioneering public health product tax s'affaiblit l'intérêt chevauchant dans high-margin UPF by internalizing health costs.
NEAK publication of diet-attributable healthcare expenditure. Hungary's health insurance system generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Réformerulation targets can reduce dépendance du système.

Document food safety violations

NÉBIH: Appliquer marketing claim standards

i: NÉBIH (Nemzeti Élelmiszerlánc-biztonsági Hivatal) — Hungary's food chain safety authority. GVH (Gazdasági Versenyhivatal) — competition authority enforcing fair advertising. Hungary transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive.
Major companies: Bonafarm Group, Pick Szeged. International companies operating in Hungary: Nestlé, Coca-Cola, PepsiCo.

NETA (népegészségügyi termékadó) — Hungary's public health product tax, one of the world's first UPF taxes, introduced 2011. NEAK (Nemzeti Egészségbiztosítási Alapkezelő) — national health insurance fund.
Országgyűlés (National Assembly of Hungary) — unicameral parliament, 199 members. Contact through parlament.hu.

ID.s: G20 économie ($1,396.3B PIB). World's largest palm oil producer (palm oil=~3.5% PIB). Significant coal (1.22% rents), oil/gas (1.61%), and exploitation minière (1.91%). Massive archipelago with extraordinary biodiversity. Palm oil expansion has driven catastrophic déforestation.

sectors.w: Indonesia produces ~60% of global palm oil. EUDR compliance will require zero-déforestation traceability to plantation level. Lorsque par centraleation forest loss data is linked to palm oil transactions, W devient calculable from commodity prices.
bp: EUDR compliance forcing zero-déforestation supply chain traceability. Lorsque international buyers require verified déforestation-free palm oil, l'intérêt chevauchant dans clearing forest for new plantations breaks — productivity improvement on existing land becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-concession environnemental data (fire occurrence, peat depth, biodiversity surveys) linked to ISPO certification. Indonesia's KLHK already monitors déforestation by satellite — publishing per-company data makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Palm oil monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, smallholder agroforestry and yield improvement on existing plantations can reduce pressure for new forest conversion.
a: Report illegal déforestation to KLHK (Ministry of Environment and Forestry)
Document land rights violations — report to Komnas HAM (National Human Rights Commission)
Report fire-setting to Satgas Karhutla (forest fire task force)
Wilmar, Golden Agri-Resources, Sinar Mas: Mettre en œuvre full NDPE (No Deforestation, No Peat, No Exploitation) commitments
Adopter RSPO certification across all operations
Investir dans yield improvement rather than area expansion
KLHK: Appliquer the permanent moratorium on new primary forest and peatland conversion
Renforcer ISPO mandatory certification with environnemental performance metrics
BPN (National Land Agency): Resolve overlapping land claims through One Map Policy
Contactez votre member of the DPR (Dewan Perwakilan Rakyat) pour exiger enforcement of the forest moratorium and peatland protection
Support EUDR implementation with adequate transition support for smallholders
Plaider via ASEAN for regional zero-déforestation commitments
Pousser pour international tropical forest conservation finance
i: KLHK (Kementerian Lingkungan Hidup dan Kehutanan) — the environment and forestry ministry, responsible for forest management, environnemental regulation, and fire prevention. Komnas HAM (Komisi Nasional Hak Asasi Manusia) — the national human rights commission. WALHI (Wahana Lingkungan Hidup Indonesia / Friends of the Earth Indonesia) — the largest environnemental NGO.

Wilmar International — the world's largest palm oil trader. Golden Agri-Resources (Sinar Mas Group) — one of the largest palm oil producers. GAPKI (Gabungan Pengusaha Kelapa Sawit Indonesia) — the Indonesian palm oil association. RSPO and ISPO (Indonesian Sustainable Huile de Palme) — certification standards.

One Map Policy (Kebijakan Satu Peta) — Indonesia's initiative to create a single, authoritative map of land use and forest cover to resolve overlapping concessions. BPDPKS (Badan Pengelola Dana Perkebunan Kelapa Sawit) — the palm oil plantation fund managing the CPO export levy.

DPR (Dewan Perwakilan Rakyat / People's Representative Council) — lower house, 580 members. DPD (Dewan Perwakilan Daerah / Regional Representative Council) — upper house, 136 members. Contact through dpr.go.id. Indonesia is a presidential démocratie and the world's third-largest démocratie.

IE.s: High-income EU économie ($609.2B PIB, inflated by multinational profit booking). Industrie manufacturière à 29.6% PIB (pharma, tech). Major corporate tax hub. Agriculture à 1% PIB but intensive dairy farming with significant methane émissions. Services à 60.6% PIB.

sectors.w: Ireland's dairy herd expansion post-milk-quota-abolition (2015) increased methane émissions by 20%+. Lorsque per-farm greenhouse gas data is published alongside output, W devient calculable from agricultural production data.
bp: Réformer CAP subsidies from production-linked to environnemental outcome-linked. Lorsque dairy expansion no longer receives per-cow support, l'intérêt chevauchant dans herd growth s'affaiblit.
Mandatory per-farm greenhouse gas balance disclosure. Ireland's Bord Bia Origin Green program collects data — making it publicly verifiable makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Ruminant digestion produces methane by biological process. Feed additives (e.g., 3-NOP) can reduce but not eliminate enteric methane.
a: Report environnemental violations to EPA Ireland
Document water pollution from dairy — report to local authority water services
Use Irish Protected Disclosures Act 2014 for protected reporting
Kerry Group, Glanbia, Ornua: Set binding methane reduction targets
Investir dans methane-reducing feed technologies
Adopter Science Based Targets for Irish dairy operations
DAFM: Réformer agricultural supports toward émissions reduction
EPA: Renforcer agricultural émissions monitoring
Bord Bia: Make Origin Green data independently verifiable
Contactez votre TD (Teachta Dála) or Senator pour exiger binding agricultural émissions reduction targets
Support stronger EU CAP conditionality on methane
Advocate for inclusion of agricultural methane in EU ETS
Pousser pour Ireland's Climate Action Plan sectoral émissions ceilings to be enforced
i: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) — Ireland's environnemental regulator. Protected Disclosures Act 2014 (amended 2022) — Ireland's lanceur d'alerte protection law, one of the strongest in Europe.

Kerry Group — major food ingredients company. Glanbia — dairy and nutrition company. Ornua (formerly Irish Dairy Board) — manages Kerrygold brand. Teagasc — the agriculture and food development authority providing research and extension.
DAFM (Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine) — Ireland's agriculture ministry. Bord Bia (Irish Food Board) — the food marketing agency running the Origin Green sustainability program.

Oireachtas (Parliament of Ireland) — bicameral: Dáil Éireann (160 TDs) and Seanad Éireann (60 Senators). Contact through oireachtas.ie. Ireland's Climate Action and Low Carbon Développerment Act (2021) sets legally binding émissions budgets.

IL.s: High-tech économie ($540.4B PIB) with dominant services (72.5% PIB). High military expenditure (8.78% PIB). Gas production from Leviathan and Tamar fields (0.43% gas rents). Major tech sector — 'Startup Nation.' Water management leader (desalination, drip irrigation).

sectors.w: Israel's Mediterranean gas fields (Leviathan, Tamar) were discovered in 2009-2010. Per-field émissions disclosure through ISA (Israel Securities Authority) listed company requirements makes W computable from production data.
bp: Export gas to replace coal in Egypt and Jordan — net émissions benefit if displacing higher-carbon sources. But this locks in gas infrastructure. Lorsque renewable alternatives become cheaper for regional electricity, the intérêt chevauchant s'affaiblit.
Mandatory par gisement émissions disclosure for Leviathan and Tamar operators. ISA reporting requirements provide the mechanism to make W computable.
Non cassable by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report environnemental violations to the Ministry of Environmental Protection
Document offshore émissions — report through Delek/Chevron compliance channels
Signaler à ISA if ESG disclosures are misleading
NewMed Energy (formerly Delek Drilling), Chevron: Set émissions reduction targets for Leviathan and Tamar
Investir dans regional énergie renouvelable integration
Adopter TCFD-aligned disclosure
Petroleum Commissioner: Require par gisement émissions monitoring
Electricity Authority: Accelerate énergie renouvelable targets (Israel aims for 30% renewable by 2030)
Ministry of Environmental Protection: Renforcer offshore environnemental standards
Contactez votre member of the Knesset pour exiger binding énergie renouvelable targets and gas émissions disclosure
Support regional energy cooperation through the Eastern Mediterranean Gas Forum (EMGF)
Advocate for Abraham Accords normalization to include énergie renouvelable cooperation with Gulf states
Pousser pour OECD environnemental performance review recommendations implementation
i: Ministry of Environmental Protection — Israel's autorité environnementale. ISA (Israel Securities Authority) — regulates disclosure for listed companies including Delek Group. Israel has lanceur d'alerte protections under the Protection of Workers (Exposure of Offenses) Law, 1997.
NewMed Energy — operates Leviathan field. Chevron — acquired Noble Energy's interest in Tamar. Israel Natural Gas Lines (INGL) — the d'État gas transmission company.

Petroleum Commissioner — within the Ministry of Energy, oversees oil and gas licensing. Electricity Authority — the independent electricity regulator. Israel's Electricity Market Réformer aims to increase competition and renewable penetration.

Knesset (כנסת) — Israel's unicameral parliament, 120 members. Contact through knesset.gov.il. Israel is an OECD member. EMGF (Eastern Mediterranean Gas Forum) — regional organization for gas market coordination.

IQ.s: Oil-dependent économie ($279.6B PIB) with 43.45% combined oil and gas rents — one of the world's highest. OPEC member producing ~4.5M bpd. Massive gas flaring (Iraq flares more gas than almost any other country). Post-conflict reconstruction ongoing.

sectors.w: Iraq flares $2.5B+ worth of associated gas annually. Capturing and monetizing flared gas makes W computable — the flared gas represents pure waste visible from satellite (World Bank GGFR data).
bp: Gas capture and monetization. Iraq wastes $2.5B/year in flared gas. Lorsque gas capture infrastructure is built, l'intérêt chevauchant dans flaring (which is cheaper short-term than processing) breaks as captured gas generates revenue.
Satellite-based flare monitoring (VIIRS nightfire data) already makes gas flaring volumes visible globally. Linking par gisement flaring data to production revenue makes W computable.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report environnemental violations through international operator compliance channels (BP, TotalEnergies, ExxonMobil)
Document gas flaring volumes — satellite data from World Bank GGFR provides independent verification
Report through Iraq EITI channels
BP (Rumaila), TotalEnergies (Halfaya), ExxonMobil (West Qurna): Investir dans associated gas capture infrastructure
Mettre en œuvre flare reduction targets
SOMO (State Organization for Marketing of Oil): Adopter émissions disclosure standards
Ministry of Oil: Appliquer gas flaring reduction targets in Technical Service Contracts
Ministry of Environment: Renforcer air quality enforcement near oil fields
EITI Iraq: Expand reporting to include flaring and émissions data
Support World Bank GGFR zero routine flaring by 2030 initiative for Iraq
Plaider via OPEC for managed transition and gas monetization investment
Pousser pour international climate finance for gas capture infrastructure
Support UN Iraq partnership on environnemental governance capacity building
i: Note: Iraq's institutional capacity for environnemental regulation is limited. The Ministry of Oil oversees petroleum operations. EITI Iraq — Iraq has been EITI compliant since 2012. World Bank Global Gas Flaring Reduction Partnership (GGFR) — monitors and reports on global gas flaring using satellite data.

BP operates Rumaila (Iraq's largest field). TotalEnergies has the $27B Gas Growth Integrated Project (GGIP) to capture associated gas. ExxonMobil, Lukoil, PetroChina — other major operators. Basra Oil Company — manages southern oil fields.

Ministry of Oil — oversees all petroleum operations and manages Technical Service Contracts (TSCs) with international operators. Ministry of Environment — responsible for environnemental policy but severely under-resourced.

Note: Iraq is a parlementaire démocratie but governance is fragmented among sectarian and ethnic power blocs. Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 329 members). International channels: OPEC membership, World Bank, UNAMI (UN Assistance Mission for Iraq), GGFR.

IR.s: Major oil and gas producer ($475.3B PIB) with 27.08% combined rents. World's second-largest natural gas reserves (after Russia). Under comprehensive international sanctions. Industrie manufacturière à 20.6% PIB. Theocratic governance limits civic channels.

sectors.w: International sanctions have already weakened the intérêt chevauchant by restricting Iran's ability to monetize oil exports at market price. Lorsque sanctions enforcement eliminates the discount market, Iran's economic model requires diversification.
bp: Sanctions enforcement. Lorsque Iran cannot sell oil at market prices, the economic case for énergie renouvelable investment (Iran has excellent solar potential) strengthens. The intérêt chevauchant in oil dependence breaks when export revenues are structurally constrained.
Per-field environnemental data is largely unavailable due to sanctions-related opacity. International satellite monitoring (methane detection) provides external data to estimate W.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report environnemental violations to DoE (Department of Environment)
Document air pollution and health impacts — Iran's cities rank among the world's most polluted
Report through international academic and research channels
NIOC: Reduce gas flaring and venting at production facilities
Investir dans domestic solar and wind energy development
Mettre en œuvre émissions monitoring for major fields
Ministry of Petroleum: Mettre en œuvre flaring reduction programs
DoE: Renforcer air quality enforcement in Tehran and major cities
Ministry of Energy: Accelerate énergie renouvelable development
Support international climate cooperation mechanisms that include Iran
Plaider via OPEC for managed transition planning
Pousser pour environnemental conditionality in any future sanctions relief negotiations
Support Iranian diaspora environnemental advocacy organizations
i: DoE (سازمان حفاظت محیط زیست / Department of Environment) — Iran's autorité environnementale, which has limited enforcement power. NIOC (National Iranian Oil Company) — the state oil company. Note: Iran has no effective lanceur d'alerte protection. Civil society is severely constrained.

NIOC — controls all upstream oil and gas operations. NIGC (National Iranian Gas Company) — manages gas production and distribution. Pars Oil and Gas Company — manages South Pars/North Dome gas field operations.

Ministry of Petroleum — oversees oil and gas policy. Iran's Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency Organization (SATBA) — responsible for énergie renouvelable development. Iran has installed some solar and wind capacity but it remains a tiny fraction of energy supply.

Note: Iran is a theocratic republic. The Islamic Consultative Assembly (Majles, 290 members) has limited power relative to the Supreme Leader and Guardian Council. International channels: OPEC, UNFCCC (Iran signed but did not ratify Paris Agreement), UN Environment Programme.

IS.s: Small advanced économie ($33.3B PIB) with 100% renewable electricity (geothermal and hydro). Pêche are 5% of PIB and the largest PST exposure — Iceland's fisheries management is globally studied but faces sustainability challenges from ocean warming.

sectors.w: Iceland's ITQ (Individual Transferable Quota) system generates detailed per-vessel catch data. Lorsque ecosystem health metrics (stock assessments, bycatch, seabed impact) are published alongside fishing revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer ITQ allocations to include ecosystem impact costs. Lorsque quota holders pay for bycatch and seabed damage, l'intérêt chevauchant dans maximum extraction s'affaiblit.
Publish per-vessel ecosystem impact data alongside catch revenue. Iceland's Marine and Freshwater Research Institute already conducts stock assessments — linking to economic data makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Fishing inherently removes biomass from marine ecosystems. However, science-based quota management can keep extraction within sustainable yield limits.
a: Report quota violations to Fiskistofa (Directorate of Pêche)
Document bycatch or illegal discarding
Use Iceland's lanceur d'alerte protections
Samherji, Íslandsbanki: Adopter sustainability-linked targets for fishing operations
Mettre en œuvre per-vessel bycatch reduction technology
Support ecosystem-based fisheries management
Ministry of Food, Agriculture and Pêche: Réformer ITQ system to include ecosystem impact costs
Marine Research Institute: Publish ecosystem health indicators alongside quota recommendations
Fiskistofa: Renforcer monitoring and enforcement

Support NEAFC (North-East Atlantic Pêche Commission) sustainable management measures
Advocate for Arctic Council fisheries governance coordination
Pousser pour inclusion of ecosystem health metrics in national accounts
i: Fiskistofa (Directorate of Pêche) — responsible for fisheries management, quota enforcement, and monitoring. Hafrannsóknastofnun (Marine and Freshwater Research Institute) — conducts stock assessments and marine research.

Samherji — Iceland's largest fishing company. Brim, HB Grandi — other major fishing companies. SFS (Samtök fyrirtækja í sjávarútvegi / Federation of Icelandic Fishing Vessel Owners) — the industry association.

Ministry of Food, Agriculture and Pêche — oversees fisheries policy. The ITQ (Individual Transferable Quota) system — Iceland's fisheries management system, one of the world's most studied, allocating catch shares that can be traded.

Alþingi (Althing) — Iceland's unicameral parliament, 63 members, one of the world's oldest legislatures (established 930 CE). Contact through althingi.is. NEAFC — the regional fisheries management organization for the Northeast Atlantic.

JG.s: British Crown Dependencies ($12.5B PIB) — Jersey and Guernsey. Major offshore financial centers (91.2% services). Financial services dominate with significant fund administration and trust structures. Not part of the UK or EU but closely aligned with UK regulatory standards.

sectors.w: Channel Islands' financial services facilitate global capital flows with lower transparence than UK mainland. Lorsque beneficial ownership data and tax transparence information are published, W devient calculable from financial intermediation data.
bp: Full beneficial ownership transparence aligned with UK Economic Crime Act requirements. Lorsque financial secrecy is eliminated, the intérêt chevauchant between service providers and opacity-seeking clients s'affaiblit.
Mandatory CRS (Common Reporting Standard) enhanced reporting. Publishing aggregate capital flow data makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Financial intermediation can relocate but the Channel Islands' proximity to London and established legal infrastructure create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to JFIU (Joint Financial Intelligence Unit) in Jersey or FIS (Financial Intelligence Service) in Guernsey
Document regulatory violations — report to JFSC or GFSC
Report through internal bank compliance channels
Mettre en œuvre enhanced beneficial ownership due diligence
Adopter ESG integration across fund and trust structures
Align with UK transparence standards ahead of regulatory mandates
JFSC/GFSC: Renforcer beneficial ownership verification

Support OECD Global Forum on Transparency standards
Advocate for Moneyval enhanced evaluation compliance
Pousser pour alignment with EU AMLD standards
i: JFSC (Jersey Services Financiers Commission) — Jersey's financial regulator. GFSC (Guernsey Services Financiers Commission) — Guernsey's financial regulator. JFIU / FIS — financial intelligence units.
Channel Islands host operations of major international banks and fund administrators. Jersey Finance and Guernsey Finance promote the financial services sectors.
JFSC — Jersey's integrated financial regulator. GFSC — Guernsey's equivalent. Both crown dependencies have their own regulatory frameworks but coordinate closely with UK authorities.

States of Jersey (49 members) and States of Guernsey (38 members) — the insular legislatures. Constitutional relationship with UK means UK Parliament can legislate for the Crown Dependencies on international obligations.

JM.s: Caribbean island économie ($22B PIB) with tourism and services dominant (60% PIB). Agriculture à 8% PIB. Bauxite/alumina industry historically significant but declining. Climate-vulnerable. High debt burden (IMF structural adjustment).

sectors.w: Jamaica's agricultural sector is dominated by sugar and banana monoculture on degraded soils. Lorsque soil health and watershed degradation data is published alongside agricultural output, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer agricultural subsidies toward crop diversification and agroforestry. Lorsque farmers receive support for diverse crops rather than monoculture sugar, the intérêt chevauchant breaks.
Publish watershed health and soil erosion data linked to agricultural practices. NEPA collects environnemental data — linking to farming output makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Tropical monoculture inherently degrades soil. Jamaica's Blue Mountain coffee model shows premium diversification is viable.
a: Report environnemental violations to NEPA (National Environment and Planning Agency)
Document agricultural chemical misuse — report to RADA (Rural Agricultural Développerment Authority)
Report through industry self-regulation channels

i: NEPA (National Environment and Planning Agency) — Jamaica's autorité environnementale. RADA — provides agricultural extension services.
Sugar Industry Authority — regulates Jamaica's sugar sector. Jamaica Producers Group — diversified agricultural company.
Ministry of Agriculture and Pêche — oversees agricultural policy. SIA — regulates the sugar industry.
Parliament of Jamaica — bicameral: House of Representatives (63 members) and Senate (21 appointed). Jamaica is a Westminster-style parlementaire démocratie.

JO.s: Water-scarce Middle Eastern économie ($53.4B PIB) with limited natural resources. Services à 60.4% PIB. High military expenditure (4.8% PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 17.7% (phosphate, potash, pharmaceuticals). Among the world's most water-stressed countries.

sectors.w: Jordan's phosphate and potash exploitation minière (Arab Potash Company, Jordan Phosphate Mines Company) depletes Dead Sea water levels and contaminates groundwater. Lorsque environnemental cost data is published alongside exploitation minière revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Internalize environnemental costs (Dead Sea decline, water depletion) into exploitation minière operations through environnemental levies. Lorsque miners pay for water extraction in one of Earth's most water-stressed countries, l'intérêt chevauchant dans water-intensive extraction s'affaiblit.
Publish par mine water consumption and Dead Sea level impact data. Jordan's water authority collects data — linking to exploitation minière output makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining inherently uses water. However, water recycling and dry processing can reduce per-ton water consumption.
a: Report environnemental violations to the Ministry of Environment
Document water extraction impacts — report to WAJ (Water Authority of Jordan)
Report through JPMC or APC compliance channels

Pousser pour OECD water governance recommendations implementation
i: Ministry of Environment — Jordan's autorité environnementale. WAJ (Water Authority of Jordan) — manages the country's severely limited water resources. Jordan has approximately 60 cubic meters of renewable water per capita — far below the 500 m³ threshold for absolute water scarcity.
JPMC — one of the world's largest phosphate producers. APC (Arab Potash Company) — extracts potash from the Dead Sea.
Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources — oversees exploitation minière policy. NRA (Natural Resources Authority) — the exploitation minière regulatory body.

Majlis al-Umma (Parliament of Jordan) — bicameral: Majlis al-Nuwab (House of Representatives, 130 members, elected) and Majlis al-Ayan (Senate, 65 appointed). Jordan is a monarchie constitutionnelle with limited democratic space.

KE.s: East Africa's largest économie ($120.3B PIB) with agriculture at 22.5% PIB (tea, coffee, horticulture). Services à 55.3%. 90%+ renewable electricity (geothermal, hydro). Vibrant tech sector (M-Pesa). Wildlife tourism. Climate-vulnerable (drought cycles).

sectors.w: Kenya's tea and horticulture sectors use intensive farming on shrinking arable land. Lorsque per-farm water and pesticide data is published alongside export revenue, W devient calculable. Kenya's M-Pesa infrastructure enables farm-level data collection.
bp: Réformer agricultural export incentives to require environnemental certification. Lorsque tea and flower exports must demonstrate sustainable water and pesticide use, l'intérêt chevauchant dans intensive monoculture s'affaiblit.
Farm-level environnemental data collection using mobile technology (Kenya's digital infrastructure is advanced). Making water and chemical use visible per farm makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Intensive horticulture inherently affects water and soil systems. However, Kenya's organic agriculture sector is growing and agroforestry can reduce dépendance du système.
a: Report environnemental violations to NEMA (National Environment Management Authority)
Document water pollution from flower farms — report to WRA (Water Resources Authority)

NEMA: Renforcer environnemental monitoring in intensive horticulture zones

i: NEMA (National Environment Management Authority) — Kenya's environnemental regulator, responsible for environnemental impact assessments, pollution control, and enforcement. WRA (Water Resources Authority) — manages water resources allocation and monitoring. PCPB (Pest Control Products Board) — regulates pesticide registration and use.

KTDA — manages tea production for ~600,000 smallholder farmers. KFC (Kenya Flower Council) — the self-regulatory body for the cut flower industry. Unilever (Lipton/Ekaterra) — major buyer of Kenyan tea.
Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock, Pêche and Cooperatives — oversees agricultural policy. AFA — regulates agricultural commodities including tea, coffee, and horticultural products.

Parliament of Kenya — bicameral: National Assembly (349 members) and Senate (67 members). Contact through parliament.go.ke. Kenya's 2010 Constitution includes strong environnemental protection provisions (Article 42 — right to a clean and healthy environment).

KG.s: Small mountainous économie ($17.5B PIB) dominated by gold exploitation minière (Kumtor mine = 10%+ of PIB, min=11.15%). Agriculture à 8.6% PIB. Remittances from Russia significant. Parliamentary démocratie (rare in Central Asia).

sectors.w: Kumtor gold mine (Centerra Gold, now nationalized) operates near glaciers — tailings and cyanide contamination affect water systems. Lorsque environnemental monitoring data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: Environmental bonds and community benefit agreements for all exploitation minière. Lorsque remediation costs are internalized, marginal mines become unprofitable.
Per-mine water quality and glacier impact monitoring published openly. Making environnemental costs visible makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining at high altitude inherently affects glacial systems. Improved tailings management can reduce impact.
