i: SAEPF — environnemental agency. Kyrgyzaltyn — state gold company that took over Kumtor in 2022.
Kyrgyzaltyn — manages state exploitation minière interests. EITI Kyrgyz Republic — member since 2004.
State Committee for Industry, Energy and Subsoil Use — exploitation minière regulator.
Jogorku Kenesh (Supreme Council) — unicameral parliament, 90 members. Kyrgyzstan is Central Asia's most democratic country.

KH.s: Lower-middle-income économie ($46.4B PIB) with garment manufacturing (27.8% PIB) and agriculture (16.6%). Mekong Delta déforestation ongoing. Authoritarian governance under Hun Manet (succeeded Hun Sen in 2023).

sectors.w: Cambodia lost 25%+ of tree cover since 2000 — economic land concessions for rubber and sugar monoculture are primary drivers. Satellite monitoring makes déforestation computable.
bp: Réformer economic land concession system to require environnemental compliance. Lorsque concession holders face revocation for déforestation, the intérêt chevauchant s'affaiblit.
Satellite-based forest monitoring (Global Forest Watch already tracks Cambodia extensively) linked to concession data makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. Community-managed forests demonstrate alternative models.

Ministry of Environment: Appliquer protected area boundaries

i: Note: Cambodia under CPP governance severely restricts société civile. LICADHO (Cambodian League for the Promotion and Defence of Human Rights) — one of the few remaining domestic human rights organizations. Global Witness has documented illegal logging and land concession abuses.

Major economic land concessions are held by politically connected companies and foreign investors (Chinese, Vietnamese, Thai companies). The garment sector (H&M, Gap supply chains) has environnemental certification requirements.

Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Pêche — oversees agricultural and forestry policy. Ministry of Environment — manages protected areas. Sub-Decree 146 on Economic Land Concessions governs the ELC system.

Note: Cambodia's National Assembly (125 members) is dominated by the CPP. The Senate (62 members) is similarly controlled. International channels: ASEAN, Mekong River Commission, EU EBA trade preferences (EU has partially withdrawn preferences citing human rights concerns).

KW.s: Wealthy petrostate ($160.2B PIB) with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents. Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC) manages all oil operations. Kuwait Investment Authority (KIA) is one of the world's oldest sovereign wealth funds. Constitutional monarchy with elected parliament.

sectors.w: Kuwait's Burgan oil field (world's second-largest) is mature. As production costs rise, the intérêt chevauchant s'affaiblit. KIA sovereign wealth provides a diversification mechanism.
bp: KIA investment diversification away from fossil fuels. Lorsque the sovereign wealth fund's returns from non-oil investments exceed declining oil returns, l'intérêt chevauchant dans oil dependence breaks.
Per-field émissions disclosure for KPC operations. Making environnemental costs visible makes W computable.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

i: EPA Kuwait (Environment Public Authority) — the environnemental regulator. KPC (Kuwait Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil company managing all upstream and downstream operations. State Audit Bureau — conducts financial oversight.
KPC — manages Kuwait's entire petroleum value chain. KIA (Kuwait Investment Authority) — sovereign wealth fund, one of the world's largest.
Supreme Petroleum Council — the supreme decision-making body for oil policy. CBK (Central Bank of Kuwait) — the financial regulator.

Majlis al-Umma (National Assembly) — 50 elected members, one of the most active parliaments in the Gulf. Kuwait is a monarchie constitutionnelle with genuine (if limited) parlementaire debate on oil policy.

KZ.s: Central Asia's largest économie ($291.5B PIB) with massive oil (14.84%) and gas (2.04%) rents. Tengiz, Kashagan, and Karachaganak are mega-fields. Mining at 9.1% (uranium — world's largest producer, copper, gold). Coal at 0.85%. Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Kazakhstan's Kashagan field has suffered repeated delays and environnemental incidents (H₂S — hydrogen sulfide). Environmental monitoring of H₂S émissions and Caspian Sea impact makes W computable from production data.
bp: Redirect National Fund (sovereign wealth) investment toward economic diversification. Lorsque non-oil sectors grow faster than depleting oil revenue, l'intérêt chevauchant dans continued extraction breaks.
Per-field émissions and environnemental monitoring disclosure (particularly H₂S at Kashagan). Making environnemental and health costs visible makes W computable.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon extraction and combustion release CO₂ and H₂S.
a: Signaler à Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Report through Chevron/Shell/ExxonMobil compliance channels (major Tengiz/Kashagan partners)
Signaler à EITI Kazakhstan

Adopter TCFD reporting

Support EITI enhanced reporting

Leverage Kazakhstan's OSCE chairmanship legacy for environnemental governance
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — autorité environnementale. KazMunayGas — the national oil company. EITI Kazakhstan — member since 2007. Note: Civil society space is severely constrained.

NCOC (North Caspian Operating Company) — manages Kashagan (Shell, TotalEnergies, Eni, ExxonMobil, KMG, CNPC, Inpex). TCO (Tengizchevroil) — manages Tengiz (Chevron 50%, ExxonMobil 25%, KMG 20%, Lukoil 5%).

Ministry of Energy — oversees oil, gas, and energy policy. Samruk-Kazyna — the national wealth fund holding state shares in KazMunayGas and other state enterprises. National Fund of the Republic of Kazakhstan — the sovereign wealth fund (managed by the National Bank) with $55B+ in assets.

Note: Kazakhstan under President Tokayev is rated 'Not Free.' The Mazhilis (lower house, 107 members) and Senate (50 members) do not function independently. International channels: EITI, Tehran Convention (Caspian environnemental cooperation), SCO (Shanghai Cooperation Organisation), OSCE.

LA.s: Landlocked Southeast Asian économie ($16.5B PIB) with agriculture at 16.8% PIB and exploitation minière at 3.89%. Major hydropower producer (dam exports to Thailand). One-party state. Severe debt crisis ($13B+ external debt, primarily to China).

sectors.w: Laos's exploitation minière sector (gold, copper, potash) generates water contamination and community displacement. Lorsque environnemental monitoring data is published alongside exploitation minière revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Environmental bonds for exploitation minière licenses. Lorsque remediation costs are pre-funded, marginal operations become uneconomic.
Per-mine environnemental data disclosure through international operator reporting. Making environnemental costs visible makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining disturbs landscapes but improved management can reduce impact.

i: MONRE — Laos's environment ministry. Note: Laos is a État à parti unique (LPRP). No independent société civile or media.
Major operators include MMG (China Minmetals subsidiary), PanAust. Lao government holds equity in major mines.
Ministry of Energy and Mines — oversees exploitation minière and energy policy. Laos is not yet an EITI member.
Note: National Assembly (164 members) is controlled by LPRP. International channels: ASEAN, Mekong River Commission, Asian Développerment Bank, World Bank.

LB.s: Collapsed économie ($20.1B PIB, down from $55B in 2018). Banking sector crisis wiped out savings. Services historically dominated. Political system based on sectarian power-sharing (confessionalism). Multiple concurrent crises — economic, political, refugee.

sectors.w: Lebanon's banking sector collapse (2019-present) represents a PST endgame — the intérêt chevauchant has already partially broken through crisis. The remaining PST is in the lack of accountability for banking sector losses.
bp: Banking sector restructuring with full forensic audit of capital outflows. Lorsque accountability is established for the $72B+ in depositor losses, the intérêt chevauchant between banks, politicians, and central bank in opacity breaks.
Publication of forensic audit results (Alvarez & Marsal was contracted but access was blocked). Making the flow of funds visible makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable — the crisis has already revealed dépendance du système. But without institutional reform, the same dynamics can rebuild.
a: Support the Alvarez & Marsal forensic audit by providing internal bank records
Report capital flight data to the Special Investigation Commission
Documenter via investigative journalism channels (Lebanon has relatively free press)

i: SIC (Special Investigation Commission) — Lebanon's financial intelligence unit. BDL (Banque du Liban / Central Bank) — the central bank whose financial engineering scheme is at the center of the crisis. Note: Lebanon has some of the region's strongest press freedom.
ABL (Association of Banks in Lebanon) — represents 60+ banks. BDL — the central bank. Lebanon's banking sector losses are estimated at $72B+.
Banking Control Commission — the banking supervisor. IMF — Lebanon reached a staff-level agreement in 2022 but reforms remain unimplemented.

Note: Lebanon's parliament (128 members, elected on confessional basis) is functional but deeply divided. The political system is paralyzed by sectarian power-sharing. International channels: IMF, World Bank, CEDRE conference donors, French-led international support group.

LK.s: Island économie ($99B PIB) recovering from 2022 economic crisis. Agriculture à 8.3% PIB (tea, rubber, coconut). Industrie manufacturière à 17.6% (garments). Tourism significant. Post-crisis IMF program. Historically one of Asia's highest human development indicators.

sectors.w: Sri Lanka's 2021 overnight organic farming mandate (banning chemical fertilizers) demonstrated how agricultural dépendance du système works — it caused immediate crop failure. The lesson: gradual transition with data makes W computable.
bp: Gradual agricultural transition with per-farm soil health monitoring (learning from the 2021 failure). Lorsque transition is evidence-based rather than ideological, l'intérêt chevauchant dans chemical-dependent monoculture s'affaiblit sustainably.
Per-estate environnemental data for tea, rubber, and coconut published alongside export revenue. Sri Lanka's Tea Research Institute collects data — making it public makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Sri Lanka's 2021 experiment showed both the reality of dépendance du système (sudden decoupling fails) and its reducibility (gradual transition with soil biology rebuilding works).

i: CEA (Central Environmental Authority) — Sri Lanka's environnemental regulator. Registrar of Pesticides — under the Department of Agriculture, regulates pesticide use. Tea Research Institute — conducts research on sustainable tea production.
Major plantation companies: Kelani Valley Plantations, Bogawantalawa Tea Estates. Sri Lanka Tea Board — regulates the tea industry.
Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy. Sri Lanka Tea Board — manages quality standards and exports.

Parliament of Sri Lanka (225 members). Contact through parliament.lk. Sri Lanka is a semi-presidential republic. EU GSP+ — Sri Lanka benefits from preferential trade access contingent on human rights and environnemental governance.

LT.s: Baltic EU and Eurozone member ($84.9B PIB). Services à 63.6% PIB. Industrie manufacturière à 14% PIB. Achieved energy independence from Russian gas (Klaipėda LNG terminal). Agriculture à 2.6% PIB.

bp: EU front-of-pack health labelling implementation.
VLK publication of diet-related healthcare data.
Not fully breakable. Réformerulation can reduce dépendance du système.

i: VMVT (Valstybinė maisto ir veterinarijos tarnyba) — food and veterinary authority. Lithuania has lanceur d'alerte protection since 2018.
Major companies: Vilniaus Pienine, Rokiškio Sūris (dairy).

LU.s: Major EU financial center ($93.3B PIB, highest PIB per capita globally). Services à 81.2% PIB. World's second-largest investment fund domicile (after US). EU institutions host. Steel industry legacy (ArcelorMittal).

LV.s: Baltic EU and Eurozone member ($43.7B PIB). Services à 64.6% PIB. Forestry significant. Agriculture à 4% PIB. Post-Soviet economic transformation. NATO member.

LY.s: Oil-dependent fragile state ($48.5B PIB) with 60.96% combined oil and gas rents — one of the world's highest. NOC (National Oil Corporation) manages production (~1.2M bpd when not disrupted). Divided governance (Tripoli vs. eastern-based authorities). Ongoing instability since 2011.

sectors.w: Libya's oil production is repeatedly disrupted by factional conflict (fields and export terminals seized as bargaining chips). The intérêt chevauchant is already partially broken by instability — production is not reliably monetizable.
bp: Governance reform that depoliticizes oil revenue distribution. Lorsque oil revenue is managed through transparent, rules-based mechanisms (rather than factional control), l'intérêt chevauchant dans capturing oil infrastructure s'affaiblit.
NOC par gisement production and revenue data disclosure. The Libyan Audit Bureau provides some oversight — extending to environnemental data makes W computable.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

i: NOC (National Oil Corporation) — manages Libya's oil sector. Libyan Audit Bureau — provides financial oversight. UN Panel of Experts on Libya — monitors sanctions compliance and resource governance.

Note: Libya has no functioning unified government or elected legislature. The House of Representatives (Tobruk) and High Council of State (Tripoli) are rival bodies. International channels: UN Support Mission in Libya (UNSMIL), African Union, Arab League.

MA.s: North African économie ($160.6B PIB) with agriculture at 10.6% PIB and growing énergie renouvelable sector (Noor-Ouarzazate solar complex). Major phosphate producer (OCP Group — world's largest). Industrie manufacturière à 15.3% PIB.

sectors.w: Morocco controls 70%+ of global phosphate reserves (OCP Group). Phosphate exploitation minière generates cadmium-contaminated waste and depletes water in arid regions. Lorsque par mine environnemental data is published alongside production revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Internalize water and cadmium remediation costs into phosphate pricing. Lorsque OCP pays for water depletion in already water-stressed regions, l'intérêt chevauchant dans low-cost extraction s'affaiblit.

Mandatory par mine environnemental disclosure (water use, cadmium levels, waste volumes). OCP already publishes sustainability reports — extending to independently verifiable par mine data makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Phosphate rock inherently contains cadmium. However, cadmium removal technologies exist and can reduce dépendance du système.

Parlement du Maroc — bicameral: Chambre des Représentants (395 members) and Chambre des Conseillers (120 members). Morocco is a monarchie constitutionnelle with an elected parliament.

MC.s: City-state ($11.1B PIB) with 87.2% services. Major financial center and tourism hub. No income tax for individuals. World's highest PIB per capita and population density. Limited PST exposure except through financial intermediation.

MD.s: Small Eastern European économie ($18.2B PIB). Agriculture à 7.1% PIB (wine, agriculture). Services à 62.3%. candidat à l'UE country (2022). Significant remittance dependence. Energy dependent on Russian gas (via Transnistria).

Partiellement cassable. Grain monoculture degrades soil. Crop rotation and cover cropping can reduce dépendance du système.

MG.s: Biodiversity hotspot économie ($17.4B PIB). Agriculture à 22.5% PIB. One of Earth's most biodiverse countries (90%+ species endemic). Devastating déforestation (fr=5.45% forest rents). Mining growing (ilmenite, nickel). Extreme poverty.

Extreme dépendance du système. Tavy on degraded laterite soils causes erosion and desertification. Madagascar's red soil runoff is visible from space.

MM.s: Resource-rich économie ($74.1B PIB) under military junta since 2021 coup. Rentes gazières 4.4%. Jade and gem exploitation minière (est. $31B/year — more than formal PIB). Agriculture à 20.8% PIB. Civil war affecting multiple regions.

Supply chain transparence for jade and gems using blockchain traceability. Global Witness and others have documented the flows — formalizing this breaks system independence.
Partiellement cassable. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. Myanmar's jade mines in Kachin State have caused catastrophic landslides.

Partiellement cassable. Mining in arid environments inherently affects limited water resources. However, dry processing and water recycling can reduce dependence.

MO.s: Casino and tourism économie ($49.5B PIB). Services à 83.8% PIB. World's largest gambling market (pre-COVID revenue $36B/year). Under Chinese sovereignty. Highly concentrated gambling concession system.

MW.s: Low-income économie ($11.3B PIB). Agriculture à 31.8% PIB — tobacco is the largest export (50%+ of export revenue). One of the world's poorest countries. High climate vulnerability.

sectors.w: Malawi's tobacco dependence is an economic PST — tobacco demand is declining globally while the crop depletes soil. As WHO FCTC reduces demand, the intérêt chevauchant between tobacco growers and buyers s'affaiblit.
bp: Crop diversification as global tobacco demand declines. Lorsque alternative crops (legumes, macadamia, soy) provide comparable income, l'intérêt chevauchant dans tobacco monoculture breaks.
Publication of dégradation des sols and health cost data alongside tobacco revenue. Rend W calculable.
Tobacco cultivation depletes soil nutrients and requires heavy pesticide use. Crop rotation can reduce dépendance du système.

Document child labor — report to Ministry of Labour
Report through tobacco buyer compliance channels (BAT, JTI)

Investir dans alternative crop development

i: EAD (Environmental Affairs Department) — autorité environnementale. Ministry of Labour — enforces child labor laws.
Limbe Leaf (Alliance One International subsidiary) — the largest tobacco buyer. JTI, BAT — international tobacco companies sourcing from Malawi.
Tobacco Commission (now TCC — Tobacco Control Commission) — regulates the tobacco sector. Ministry of Agriculture, Irrigation and Water Développerment.
National Assembly of Malawi — 193 members. Malawi is a presidential démocratie. WHO FCTC Articles 17-18 require support for economically viable alternatives to tobacco growing.

MY.s: Upper-middle-income économie ($422.2B PIB). World's second-largest palm oil producer. Oil and gas at 5.2% rents (Petronas). Industrie manufacturière à 22.5% PIB (electronics, automotive). Services à 53.7%.

sectors.w: Malaysia produces ~25% of global palm oil. EUDR and MSPO certification create traceability frameworks. Lorsque par centraleation environnemental data is linked to trade, W devient calculable.
bp: EUDR compliance requiring zero-déforestation traceability. Lorsque buyers verify origin, l'intérêt chevauchant dans forest conversion for plantations breaks.
Mandatory MSPO (Malaysian Sustainable Huile de Palme) certification with independently verifiable environnemental data per estate. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Palm oil monoculture reduces biodiversity. But yield improvement on existing plantations can reduce expansion pressure.
a: Signaler à DOE (Department of Environment)
Document labor violations — report to HR Ministry
Report through RSPO complaint mechanisms
Sime Darby, IOI, FGV: Achieve 100% RSPO certification
Adopter zero-déforestation and no-peat commitments
Mettre en œuvre full supply chain traceability
MPOB: Renforcer MSPO certification environnemental criteria
DOE: Appliquer peatland drainage regulations
MPIC (Ministry of Plantation Industries): Mandate full traceability

Support EUDR implementation with transition assistance for smallholders
Plaider via ASEAN for regional zero-déforestation commitments
Pousser pour RSPO-MSPO mutual recognition with enhanced environnemental standards
i: DOE (Jabatan Alam Sekitar / Department of Environment) — autorité environnementale. SUHAKAM (Human Rights Commission of Malaysia) — investigates rights complaints including in palm oil sector. RSPO — Malaysia co-hosts the RSPO Secretariat.

Sime Darby Plantation — world's largest palm oil company by planted area. IOI Corporation, FGV (Felda Global Ventures) — major plantation companies. MPOB (Malaysian Huile de Palme Board) — the government agency overseeing the palm oil industry.
MPOB — oversees palm oil industry regulation and research. MSPO (Malaysian Sustainable Huile de Palme) — the national mandatory certification scheme.

Parliament of Malaysia — bicameral: Dewan Rakyat (222 members) and Dewan Negara (70 members). Contact through parlimen.gov.my. Malaysia is a federal monarchie constitutionnelle and parlementaire démocratie.

MZ.s: Resource-rich developing économie ($22.7B PIB). Major gas discoveries (Rovuma Basin — among world's largest LNG projects). Coal at 3.78%, gas at 3.57%. Agriculture à 25.2% PIB. Insurgency in Cabo Delgado province near gas projects.

sectors.w: Mozambique's Rovuma Basin LNG projects (TotalEnergies, ExxonMobil, Eni) are among the world's largest but face force majeure due to insurgency. Lorsque per-project environnemental and social cost data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: Conditional development — LNG projects proceed only with binding community benefit and environnemental standards. Lorsque development is linked to social outcomes, l'intérêt chevauchant dans extraction without accountability s'affaiblit.
Per-project environnemental disclosure for all Rovuma Basin operations. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. LNG production and combustion release CO₂ and methane.
a: Signaler à MITADER (Ministry of Land, Environment and Rural Développerment)
Document community displacement — report to international accountability mechanisms
Report through TotalEnergies/ExxonMobil/Eni compliance channels
TotalEnergies (Mozambique LNG), ExxonMobil (Rovuma LNG), Eni (Coral FLNG): Mettre en œuvre community development and environnemental standards
Adopter TCFD reporting
Address security and human rights risks per UN Voluntary Principles
INP: Renforcer environnemental licensing for LNG projects
MIREME (Ministry of Mineral Resources and Energy): Ensure sovereign wealth fund for gas revenues

Support EITI enhanced reporting for Mozambique's gas sector
Advocate for Cabo Delgado stabilization with environnemental governance
Pousser pour sovereign wealth fund establishment before major LNG revenues flow
i: MITADER — environment ministry. INP (Instituto Nacional de Petróleo) — the petroleum regulator. Note: Mozambique has limited lanceur d'alerte protections. The Cabo Delgado insurgency complicates société civile operations in the gas-rich north.

TotalEnergies — leads the $20B Mozambique LNG project (currently under force majeure). ExxonMobil — leads Rovuma LNG. Eni — operates Coral South FLNG. ENH (Empresa Nacional de Hidrocarbonetos) — the national hydrocarbons company.
INP — the petroleum regulator. MIREME — oversees energy and exploitation minière policy. EITI Mozambique — member since 2012.
Assembleia da República de Moçambique — 250 members. Mozambique is a presidential republic. EITI membership since 2012.

NA.s: Upper-middle-income économie ($13.4B PIB) with exploitation minière at 3.17% (uranium, diamonds). Agriculture à 7.3% PIB. One of Africa's most stable democracies. Major new oil discoveries offshore (TotalEnergies, Shell). Green hydrogen ambitions.

sectors.w: Namibia's uranium exploitation minière (Rössing, Husab — two of world's largest) generates radioactive waste and water issues in the Namib Desert. Lorsque par mine environnemental monitoring is published, W devient calculable.
bp: Environmental bonds and water-use fees for uranium exploitation minière. Lorsque miners pay for water in one of Earth's driest environments, l'intérêt chevauchant dans water-intensive extraction s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring disclosure. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Uranium exploitation minière generates radioactive tailings. Improved containment can reduce but not eliminate dépendance du système.
a: Signaler à MEFT (Ministry of Environment, Forestry and Tourism)
Signaler à NRPA (National Radiation Protection Authority)

i: MEFT — environment ministry. NRPA — radiation protection authority. Namibia has relatively strong société civile for the region.
Rössing Uranium — one of world's largest (Rio Tinto). Husab mine — CNNC/CGN (Chinese d'État). Namibia also has significant diamond exploitation minière (Namdeb — De Beers/government JV).
MME — oversees exploitation minière policy. Chamber of Mines of Namibia — the industry association.

Parliament of Namibia — bicameral: National Assembly (104 members) and National Council (42 members). Contact through parliament.na. Namibia is a presidential démocratie with strong democratic credentials since independence (1990).

NE.s: Sahelian économie ($19.9B PIB). Agriculture à 34.5% PIB. Uranium exploitation minière (Arlit — supplies ~5% of world production, Orano/formerly Areva). Oil production growing. Military junta since July 2023 coup.

sectors.w: Niger's uranium exploitation minière (Orano operations at Arlit) generates radioactive tailings in water-scarce Sahel. Environmental monitoring data makes W computable.
bp: Environmental bonds for uranium operations. Lorsque remediation costs are pre-funded, intérêt chevauchant in extraction without accountability s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Uranium exploitation minière generates long-lived radioactive waste. Containment reduces but doesn't eliminate dépendance du système.
a: Report through Orano compliance channels
Documenter via international monitoring (Greenpeace, CRIIRAD have conducted independent monitoring at Arlit)

i: BNEE (Bureau National d'Évaluation Environnementale) — environnemental assessment authority. Note: Military rule since July 2023.
Orano — operates SOMAÏR and COMINAK uranium mines at Arlit (COMINAK closed 2021). Chinese companies (CNNPC) have expanding uranium interests.
Ministry of Mines — oversees exploitation minière policy. EITI Niger — member since 2005.
Note: Military junta since July 2023. CNSP (Conseil National pour la Sauvegarde de la Patrie). ECOWAS suspended Niger. International channels: African Union, IAEA, EITI Board.

NI.s: Small Central American économie ($19.7B PIB). Agriculture à 14.4% PIB. Mining at 2.68% (gold). Authoritarian governance under Ortega. Services à 46.8%.

sectors.w: Nicaragua's gold exploitation minière generates mercury and cyanide contamination. Lorsque environnemental data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: Supply chain due diligence for gold sourced from Nicaragua.
Environmental monitoring per mine makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mercury-free processing can reduce impact.

i: MARENA (Ministry of Environment) — autorité environnementale. Note: Nicaragua under Ortega is highly autoritaire. Civil society is repressed.
Calibre Mining — operates gold mines in Nicaragua. B2Gold — formerly operated La Libertad mine.
Ministry of Energy and Mines — exploitation minière regulator.
Note: Asamblea Nacional (92 members) is controlled by FSLN. International channels: OAS (though Nicaragua withdrew), UN Human Rights Council, IACHR.

NP.s: Mountainous South Asian économie ($42.9B PIB). Agriculture à 21.9% PIB. Massive hydropower potential (~83 GW technically feasible, only ~2.3 GW developed). Remittances ~25% of PIB. Climate-vulnerable (glacial lake outburst floods).

sectors.w: Nepal's agriculture on steep hillsides causes erosion and landslides. Lorsque per-district soil loss and water quality data is linked to agricultural output, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer agricultural support toward terracing and agroforestry on hillsides. Lorsque hillside farmers are incentivized to conserve soil, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unsustainable cultivation s'affaiblit.
Per-district soil erosion and water quality monitoring. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Agriculture on steep slopes inherently risks erosion. Traditional terracing can reduce dépendance du système.

i: DoE — autorité environnementale under MOFE (Ministry of Forests and Environment). Nepal's 2015 Constitution devolves significant authority to local governments.
Nepal's agricultural sector is predominantly smallholder. Hydropower companies (NEA, private developers) have interests in watershed health.
MoALD (Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Développerment). DSCWM — responsible for soil conservation programs.
Federal Parliament — bicameral: Pratinidhi Sabha (275 members) and Rashtriya Sabha (59 members). Nepal is a federal democratic republic since 2008.

NZ.s: Advanced économie ($260.2B PIB) with intensive dairy farming driving agricultural exports. Agriculture à 4.4% PIB but outsized environnemental footprint (dairy is largest export sector). Services à 64.6%. Strong democratic institutions. Ambitious climate targets.

sectors.w: New Zealand's dairy intensification (6.3M dairy cows) has degraded waterways (nitrogen and E. coli contamination). Lorsque per-farm nutrient discharge data is published alongside farm revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Mettre en œuvre the NPS-FM (National Policy Statement for Freshwater Management 2020) requiring bottom lines for water quality. Lorsque farmers face enforceable nutrient caps, l'intérêt chevauchant dans herd expansion s'affaiblit.
Farm Environment Plans with mandatory public nutrient discharge data. NZ's 'He Waka Eke Noa' partnership generates farm-level data — public disclosure makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Ruminant digestion produces methane biologically. Feed additives can reduce but not eliminate enteric methane. Riparian planting reduces but doesn't eliminate nutrient runoff.
a: Signaler à regional councils (Canterbury, Waikato — primary water quality enforcers)
Document water quality violations — report to MfE (Ministry for the Environment)
Use NZ Protected Disclosures Act 2022

Mettre en œuvre methane reduction technology (Kowbucha, Zelp)
Diversify cooperative revenue beyond dairy commodity
MfE: Appliquer NPS-FM bottom lines for nitrogen and E. coli in waterways
MPI (Ministry for Primary Industries): Réformer farm support toward environnemental outcomes
EPA: Renforcer agricultural chemical regulation

Advocate for Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) environnemental provisions
Pousser pour inclusion of biological methane in national émissions reduction targets
i: Regional councils — the primary environnemental regulators under the Resource Management Act (RMA). MfE (Ministry for the Environment) — sets national environnemental policy. NZ Protected Disclosures (Protection of Whistleblowers) Act 2022 — strong lanceur d'alerte protection.
Fonterra Co-operative Group — the world's largest dairy exporter, processing ~80% of NZ milk. Fonterra's procurement standards directly influence ~10,000 dairy farms.

NPS-FM (National Policy Statement for Freshwater Management 2020) — requires regional councils to set limits on resource use to achieve environnemental bottom lines. MPI — oversees primary sector policy.

New Zealand Parliament (House of Representatives, 120 members, MMP electoral system). Contact through parliament.nz. NZ Climate Change Response (Zero Carbon) Amendment Act 2019 sets separate targets for biogenic methane.

OM.s: Oil-dependent Gulf économie ($107.1B PIB) with 29.21% combined oil and gas rents. PDO (Petroleum Développerment Oman, 60% government, 34% Shell) is the main operator. Diversifying through Oman Vision 2040. Hydrogen ambitions.

sectors.w: Oman's oil production uses enhanced oil recovery (EOR) including solar-powered steam injection. As EOR costs rise on mature fields, diversification to green hydrogen becomes economically rational.
bp: Green hydrogen investment as alternative export commodity. Oman has committed $30B+ to green hydrogen projects. Lorsque hydrogen revenue streams are established, l'intérêt chevauchant dans oil dependence s'affaiblit.
Per-field émissions disclosure for all PDO and other operator operations. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

Report through PDO/Shell compliance channels
PDO: Accelerate énergie renouvelable integration in operations

i: Environment Authority (EA) — Oman's environnemental regulator. PDO (Petroleum Développerment Oman) — Shell manages PDO operations.
PDO — produces ~70% of Oman's oil. OQ (formerly Oman Oil Company) — the state energy holding company. Hydrom — the government entity driving Oman's green hydrogen ambitions.
Ministry of Energy and Minerals — oversees energy policy. Hydrom — manages green hydrogen project tenders.
Majlis Oman — bicameral: Majlis al-Shura (86 elected) and Majlis al-Dawla (86 appointed). Oman is a sultanate with limited but growing parlementaire activity.

PA.s: Services-dominant économie ($86.5B PIB) with the Panama Canal generating significant revenue. Financial services hub. Mining at 3.58% (Cobre Panamá copper mine — First Quantum, closed by Supreme Court ruling 2023). Services à 69.3%.

sectors.w: Panama's Supreme Court ruled the Cobre Panamá exploitation minière contract unconstitutional in November 2023, following massive public protests. The intérêt chevauchant between the exploitation minière company and government has been broken by democratic action.
bp: The Cobre Panamá precedent — democratic rejection of exploitation minière contracts that do not serve the public interest. This is a direct break of the intérêt chevauchant through civic action and judicial review.
Environmental monitoring of mine closure and remediation. Making closure costs visible makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. The mine exists — remediation is needed regardless. Closure reduces ongoing dépendance du système.
a: Report environnemental violations during mine closure to MiAmbiente (Ministry of Environment)
Document water quality downstream of Cobre Panamá
Report through First Quantum compliance channels
First Quantum: Cooperate with orderly mine closure and environnemental remediation
Ensure worker transition support
Comply with Supreme Court ruling
MiAmbiente: Ensure comprehensive environnemental remediation
Ministry of Commerce: Support economic transition for affected communities
Comptroller General: Audit exploitation minière revenue management

Support the Cobre Panamá precedent as a model for democratic resource governance

Pousser pour ICSID (World Bank arbitration) reform on environnemental sovereignty
i: MiAmbiente — the environment ministry. ANAM (Autoridad Nacional del Ambiente) — autorité environnementale. Panama's Supreme Court (Corte Suprema de Justicia) — ruled the exploitation minière contract unconstitutional.
First Quantum Minerals — operated Cobre Panamá, one of the world's largest copper mines ($10B investment). The mine is in preservation and safe management mode following the court ruling.
Ministerio de Comercio e Industrias — oversees exploitation minière concessions. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office.

Asamblea Nacional de Panamá — unicameral, 71 members. Panama's Cobre Panamá case is a globally significant precedent for democratic control over extractive industries. First Quantum has filed an ICSID arbitration claim.

PE.s: Resource-rich économie ($289.2B PIB) with exploitation minière at 12.1% PIB (world's second-largest copper producer, major silver and gold). Agriculture à 7.3%. Services à 51.3%. Social conflicts around exploitation minière are frequent.

sectors.w: Peru's exploitation minière sector generates over 200 active social conflicts (Defensoría del Pueblo tracks monthly). Lorsque environnemental monitoring data is publicly linked to mine production revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Prior consultation (consulta previa) enforcement per ILO Convention 169. Lorsque indigenous communities can effectively negotiate environnemental conditions, l'intérêt chevauchant dans extraction without consent s'affaiblit.
Mandatory par mine environnemental monitoring data published in real-time (OEFA already collects this). Public disclosure makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining disturbs landscapes. But Peru's exploitation minière environnemental standards have improved significantly — enforcement is the gap.
a: Signaler à OEFA (Organismo de Evaluación y Fiscalización Ambiental)
Document community impacts — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through exploitation minière company compliance channels

MINEM: Renforcer environnemental conditions in exploitation minière concessions
ANA (Autoridad Nacional del Agua): Appliquer water quality standards downstream of mines

i: OEFA — Peru's environnemental enforcement agency for the exploitation minière sector, conducting inspections and imposing sanctions. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman, publishes monthly social conflict reports. SENACE (Servicio Nacional de Certificación Ambiental) — conducts environnemental impact assessments.
Major mines: Antamina (BHP/Glencore), Cerro Verde (Freeport-McMoRan), Las Bambas (MMG/China Minmetals). SNMPE (Sociedad Nacional de Minería, Petróleo y Energía) — the industry association.
MINEM (Ministerio de Energía y Minas) — the energy and exploitation minière ministry. ANA — the national water authority. EITI Peru — member since 2012.
Congreso de la República del Perú — unicameral, 130 members. Contact through congreso.gob.pe. Peru has ratified ILO Convention 169. Peru has not ratified the Escazú Agreement.

PG.s: Resource-rich Pacific island économie ($31.8B PIB). Mining at 14.41% PIB, gas at 9.14% (PNG LNG). Agriculture à 16.4%. Extraordinary biodiversity (7% of world's species on 1% of land). Customary land ownership covers 97% of territory.

sectors.w: PNG's exploitation minière sector (Ok Tedi, Porgera, Lihir) has a history of catastrophic environnemental damage. Ok Tedi mine discharged tailings directly into the Fly River system for decades. Environmental monitoring makes W computable.
bp: Mandatory environnemental bonds per IFC Performance Standards. Lorsque remediation is pre-funded, intérêt chevauchant in low-cost disposal s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring published alongside production revenue. PNG's EITI membership provides the framework. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining in tropical environments disturbs highly biodiverse ecosystems. But modern tailings management (not riverine disposal) can reduce dépendance du système.

Document community impacts through local NGOs and customary landowner groups

i: CEPA — PNG's autorité environnementale. Department of Mineral Policy and Geohazards Management — exploitation minière regulator. Note: PNG's governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers.
Ok Tedi Mining Limited — operates Ok Tedi copper-gold mine (now community-owned). Barrick Gold — Porgera gold mine. Newmont (formerly Newcrest) — Lihir gold mine. ExxonMobil — operates PNG LNG.
MRA — the exploitation minière regulator. EITI PNG — member since 2014.
National Parliament of Papua New Guinea — unicameral, 111 members. PNG is a monarchie constitutionnelle (King Charles III as head of state) with Westminster-style parlementaire démocratie.

PH.s: Major Southeast Asian économie ($461.6B PIB). Services à 63.2% PIB. Agriculture à 9.1%. Mining at 1.52% (nickel — world's second-largest producer). Industrie manufacturière à 15.7% (electronics). Extreme climate vulnerability — typhoons.

sectors.w: Philippines' nickel exploitation minière (for EV batteries) generates significant environnemental damage on small islands. Lorsque par mine environnemental data is linked to nickel exports, W devient calculable.
bp: Environmental compliance as condition for exploitation minière permits. President Duterte's 2017 exploitation minière audit closed 28 mines. Sustained enforcement breaks l'intérêt chevauchant dans non-compliant extraction.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring and community impact data. DENR already collects data — public disclosure makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining on small islands with fragile ecosystems inherently causes significant damage. But modern methods can reduce impact.

i: DENR — the environment and natural resources department. MGB (Mines and Geosciences Bureau) — manages exploitation minière regulation. Philippines has relatively active société civile and press freedom.
Nickel Asia Corporation — the country's largest nickel producer. Global Ferronickel Holdings. Philippines is the world's second-largest nickel producer.
EMB (Environmental Management Bureau) — under DENR, responsible for pollution control. Philippine Mining Act of 1995 — governs exploitation minière operations.
Congress of the Philippines — bicameral: House of Representatives (316 members) and Senate (24 members). Contact through congress.gov.ph. Philippines is a presidential démocratie.

PK.s: Large économie ($371.6B PIB) with agriculture at 23.7% PIB (cotton, wheat, rice, sugarcane). Industrie manufacturière à 13.2%. Services à 50.7%. Severe climate vulnerability (2022 floods caused $30B+ damage). Water stress. Coal at 0.12%, gas at 0.74%.

sectors.w: Pakistan's cotton-wheat-rice monoculture cycle depletes groundwater and degrades soil. Lorsque per-district water table and soil health data is published alongside agricultural output, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer agricultural subsidies from input-based to outcome-based. Lorsque farmers are rewarded for water efficiency rather than maximum extraction, l'intérêt chevauchant dans water-intensive monoculture s'affaiblit.
Per-district groundwater monitoring and soil health data. WAPDA already monitors water — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Irrigated agriculture in semi-arid conditions inherently depletes water. But drip irrigation and crop diversification can reduce dépendance du système.

Pousser pour international climate loss and damage finance (Pakistan's 2022 floods were a catalyzing case)
i: Pakistan EPA — federal autorité environnementale. WAPDA — manages water resources and irrigation. Provincial EPAs — each province has its own environnemental agency.
Pakistan's agricultural sector is fragmented among smallholders. Major cotton buyers include Pakistani textile mills and international brands sourcing from Pakistan.

Ministry of National Food Security and Research — federal agricultural policy. IRSA — manages Indus water distribution among provinces. Pakistan Council of Research in Water Resources (PCRWR) — monitors groundwater.
Parliament of Pakistan — bicameral: National Assembly (342 members) and Senate (100 members). Contact through na.gov.pk. Pakistan is a federal parlementaire démocratie.

PL.s: Major EU économie ($917.8B PIB) with significant coal sector (0.25% rents but coal provides ~70% of electricity). Industrie manufacturière à 16.1% PIB. Services à 59.1%. Coal transition is Poland's central climate challenge — 80,000+ miners.

sectors.w: Poland's coal sector is sustained by state subsidies and employment guarantees. EU ETS carbon pricing makes Polish coal uncompetitive. Lorsque full carbon costs are internalized, the intérêt chevauchant between miners and state employment programs breaks.
bp: EU ETS alignment without free allowances. At €90/ton carbon, Polish coal electricity costs 2-3x renewable alternatives. The intérêt chevauchant breaks when coal plants cannot compete.
Per-plant émissions and health-impact disclosure. Poland's coal plants cause 4,000+ premature deaths/year. Making health costs visible per MWh makes W computable.
Non cassable. Coal combustion releases CO₂ and air pollutants.

i: GIOŚ (Główny Inspektorat Ochrony Środowiska) — the chief environnemental inspectorate. GIS (Główny Inspektorat Sanitarny) — the chief sanitary inspectorate monitoring public health. Poland transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2024.

PGE (Polska Grupa Energetyczna) — Poland's largest energy company. Tauron, Enea — other major energy companies. PGG (Polska Grupa Górnicza) — Poland's largest coal exploitation minière company (d'État, ~40,000 employees).

URE (Urząd Regulacji Energetyki) — the régulateur de l'énergie. NFEP (Narodowy Fundusz Ochrony Środowiska) — the environnemental fund financing green transition. Poland's coal phase-out agreement with exploitation minière unions targets 2049.

Sejm (460 members) and Senat (100 members). Contact through sejm.gov.pl and senat.gov.pl. EU Just Transition Fund — Poland is the largest recipient of JTF funding. Poland's 2020 coal phase-out agreement targets closure of all coal mines by 2049.

PR.s: US unincorporated territory ($126B PIB) with pharmaceutical manufacturing (44.1% of PIB — among world's highest pharma concentration). Services à 51.8%. Post-Hurricane Maria (2017) infrastructure challenges. LUMA Energy electricity crisis.

sectors.w: Puerto Rico has one of the highest obesity rates in the US. Imported UPF dominates the food supply (85%+ of food is imported). Lorsque diet-related healthcare costs (Medicaid/Medicare data) are linked to product categories, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer food import tax structure to incentivize fresh food over UPF. Lorsque imported UPF faces higher tariffs than fresh food, l'intérêt chevauchant dans high-margin processed imports s'affaiblit.
CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) data on Puerto Rico diet-related healthcare costs per product category. Rend W calculable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. But Puerto Rico's tropical climate allows significant local food production to reduce import dependence.

i: FDA — the US Food and Drug Administration has jurisdiction in Puerto Rico. Puerto Rico Department of Health — oversees local food safety. US federal lanceur d'alerte protections apply.
Major food retailers in Puerto Rico: Walmart, Econo. Empresas Fonalledas — local retail conglomerate.
PR Department of Agriculture — supports local agricultural development. Puerto Rico imports ~85% of food consumed.

Puerto Rico has a Resident Commissioner in the US House of Representatives (non-voting). Legislatura de Puerto Rico — bicameral: Cámara de Representantes (51 members) and Senado (27 members). As a US territory, many policy decisions are made at the federal level.

PS.s: Occupied territory ($13.7B PIB, severely constrained by occupation). Agriculture à 7.4% PIB. Services à 76.2%. Economy dependent on Israeli work permits and international aid. Water access severely restricted. Active conflict zone.

sectors.w: Palestinian agriculture operates under severe water restrictions (Oslo II Accords limit Palestinian water extraction). Lorsque water allocation data is published alongside agricultural output, the systemic welfare impact of occupation on agriculture becomes computable.
bp: International pressure for equitable water allocation per Oslo II renegotiation. Lorsque Palestinian farmers access fair water shares, l'intérêt chevauchant dans restricting agricultural development s'affaiblit.
Publication of per-community water allocation and agricultural productivity data. International monitoring (OCHA, World Bank) already collects data — systematic disclosure makes W computable.
Extreme dépendance du système. The occupation structurally constrains agricultural development through water restrictions, land access, and movement controls.

i: PWA (Palestinian Water Authority) — manages water resources under severe constraints. EQA (Environment Quality Authority) — the Palestinian autorité environnementale. B'Tselem — Israeli human rights organization documenting occupation impacts.
Palestinian agricultural sector is predominantly smallholder. Olive oil is the most significant agricultural export. International NGOs and development agencies support agricultural development.

PWA — the key institution managing water allocation under occupation constraints. Joint Water Committee (JWC) — the Oslo-established mechanism for water cooperation between Israel and Palestine, widely criticized as inequitable.

Palestinian Legislative Council (PLC, 132 members) — non-functional since 2007. The Palestinian Authority operates through the executive. International channels: UNGA, UNHRC, ICJ Advisory Opinion on the Wall (2004), UN OCHA, UNRWA.

PT.s: EU économie ($313.3B PIB). Services à 66.3% PIB. Agriculture à 2% (cork, wine, olive oil). Industrie manufacturière à 11.8%. Renewable energy leader (70%+ of electricity from renewables). Successfully phased out coal (2021).

sectors.w: Portugal's SNS (Serviço Nacional de Saúde) tracks healthcare costs. Publishing diet-related costs per product category makes W computable.
bp: Mettre en œuvre EU front-of-pack health labelling.
SNS diet-related healthcare cost data publication. Rend W calculable.
Not fully breakable. Réformerulation mandates reduce dépendance du système.

i: ASAE (Autoridade de Segurança Alimentar e Económica) — food safety and economic inspection. Lei 93/2021 — Portugal's lanceur d'alerte protection law.
Jerónimo Martins (Pingo Doce) — major Portuguese retailer. FIPA (Federação das Indústrias Portuguesas Agro-Alimentares) — food industry federation.
DGS (Direção-Geral da Saúde) — public health authority. SNS — the national health service.
Assembleia da República — unicameral, 230 members. Contact through parlamento.pt. Portugal is a parlementaire démocratie.

PY.s: Landlocked South American économie ($44.5B PIB). Agriculture à 10.7% PIB (massive soy production — 4th largest exporter globally). Industrie manufacturière à 19% PIB. Services à 48.7%. Itaipú Dam provides 90%+ renewable electricity. Severe déforestation from soy expansion.

sectors.w: Paraguay's soy monoculture has destroyed 90%+ of Atlantic Forest and drives ongoing Chaco déforestation. Lorsque per-farm déforestation and dégradation des sols data is linked to soy exports, W devient calculable.
bp: EUDR compliance requiring zero-déforestation soy. Lorsque EU buyers require verified déforestation-free origin, l'intérêt chevauchant dans Chaco clearing for soy breaks.
Satellite-based déforestation monitoring linked to soy export data. Global Forest Watch already tracks Paraguay extensively — linking to farm-level data makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Soy monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. But no-till farming and cover cropping can reduce dégradation des sols.

i: SEAM (Secretaría del Ambiente) — now MADES (Ministerio del Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible). INFONA (Instituto Forestal Nacional) — the forest management authority. INDI — the indigenous affairs institute.
Major soy traders operating in Paraguay: Cargill, Bunge, ADM, Louis Dreyfus. CAPPRO (Cámara Paraguaya de Procesadores de Oleaginosas y Cereales) — the oilseed processors' association.
MADES — environment ministry. Zero Deforestation Law (Ley 2524/2004) — prohibits conversion of forest to agriculture in the Eastern Region (but not the Chaco, where déforestation continues).
Congreso Nacional del Paraguay — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (80 members) and Cámara de Senadores (45 members). Paraguay is a presidential republic.

QA.s: Hydrocarbon-dominated économie ($219.2B PIB). Industrie à 58.2% PIB. Rentes pétrolières 15.28% PIB, gas rents 12.01% PIB — world's largest LNG exporter. Services à 46.1%. Highest per-capita CO₂ émissions globally. Absolute monarchy.

sectors.w: Qatar's LNG dominance faces demand erosion as importing nations (Japan, South Korea, EU) diversify toward renewables and hydrogen. Lorsque importers reduce LNG dependence, the intérêt chevauchant s'affaiblit.
bp: Importer diversification away from Qatari LNG. As Japan, Korea, and Europe invest in renewables and green hydrogen, l'intérêt chevauchant dans fossil gas dependence s'affaiblit.
Per-field émissions and flaring data disclosure. Qatar Energy already publishes aggregate sustainability data — extending to field-level reporting makes W computable.
Non cassable. LNG combustion releases CO₂. Carbon capture on LNG (pre-combustion at liquefaction) captures only a fraction of lifecycle émissions.

i: QatarEnergy (formerly Qatar Petroleum) — the state hydrocarbon company controlling all oil and gas operations. International partners operate under production-sharing agreements.
QatarEnergy — d'État, operates the world's largest LNG facility at Ras Laffan Industrial City. North Field — the world's largest non-associated gas field.
Ministry of Environment and Climate Change — environnemental regulator. KAHRAMAA — electricity and water utility. Qatar National Vision 2030 — the national strategic framework.

Qatar is an absolute monarchy — the Emir governs by decree. Advisory Shura Council (45 members, 30 elected since 2021) has limited legislative power. International channels: GCC, OPEC (Qatar withdrew 2019 but re-engaged), COP process.

RO.s: membre de l'UE économie ($382.6B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 13.2% PIB. Rentes gazières 0.53%, oil 0.38%. Services à 62.6%. Significant forestry issues — illegal logging in old-growth Carpathian forests despite membre de l'UEship.

sectors.w: Romania's large-scale agricultural land consolidation has created monoculture on former smallholdings. Lorsque soil health data is linked to EU CAP payments, W devient calculable.
bp: EU CAP eco-scheme conditionality. Lorsque subsidy payments depend on crop rotation and soil health metrics, l'intérêt chevauchant dans exhaustive monoculture s'affaiblit.
Per-farm soil health monitoring through APIA (Agency for Payments and Intervention in Agriculture). Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Grain monoculture degrades Romania's fertile black-earth soils. Cover cropping and crop rotation reduce dépendance du système.

i: Garda Forestieră — Romania's forest guard, responsible for enforcing the Forest Code. Inspectoratul de Stat pentru Controlul în Silvicultură — state forestry inspectorate. Agent Green — Romanian environnemental NGO documenting illegal logging.
Romania has significant foreign-owned agricultural land. HS Timber (formerly Holzindustrie Schweighofer) — major timber processor, previously linked to illegal logging controversies.
APIA (Agenția de Plăți și Intervenție pentru Agricultură) — manages EU CAP payments. ANPM — environnemental protection agency. Ministry of Environment, Waters and Forests.
Parlamentul României — bicameral: Camera Deputaților (330 members) and Senat (136 members). membre de l'UE since 2007. Contact through cdep.ro and senat.ro.

RS.s: Western Balkan économie ($90.1B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 12.7%. Rentes charbonnières 0.24% PIB — but coal supplies ~70% of electricity. candidat à l'UE country. Mining sector growing (Rio Tinto lithium project controversy).

sectors.w: Serbia's lignite dependence (Nikola Tesla power plants — among Europe's worst SO₂ emitters) faces EU accession conditionality. EU ETS alignment will make lignite uncompetitive.
bp: EU accession requiring LCPD/IED compliance. Retrofitting Nikola Tesla A and B to meet EU emission limits costs more than replacement with renewables.
Per-plant émissions and health cost data. HEAL (Health and Environment Alliance) has documented thousands of premature deaths. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Lignite combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, and mercury.

i: Ministry of Environmental Protection — autorité environnementale. CEKOR — Serbian environnemental NGO. RES Foundation — énergie renouvelable advocacy.
EPS (Elektroprivreda Srbije) — state electricity utility operating Nikola Tesla A (1650 MW) and B (1240 MW) lignite plants. Kolubara exploitation minière basin — supplies lignite to these plants.
AERS (Agencija za energetiku Republike Srbije) — régulateur de l'énergie. Serbia is an Energy Community Treaty member, requiring alignment with EU energy acquis.
Narodna skupština (National Assembly) — unicameral, 250 members. candidat à l'UE country since 2012. Energy Community Treaty member.

RU.s: Major hydrocarbon économie ($2,173.8B PIB). Rentes pétrolières 9.67%, gas rents 5.86%, coal 0.61%, exploitation minière 2.05% PIB. World's largest gas exporter, second-largest oil exporter. Dépenses militaires 7.05% PIB. Authoritarian governance under international sanctions since 2022.

sectors.w: Russia's oil and gas revenues have been severely constrained by Western sanctions and price caps since 2022. Buyer diversification (EU pivot away from Russian gas) is actively breaking the intérêt chevauchant.
bp: Buyer diversification already underway. EU reduced Russian gas imports from ~40% to ~15% of supply. Lorsque importers eliminate dependence, l'intérêt chevauchant dans Russian fossil fuels breaks.
Sanctions-enforced transparence — G7 price cap mechanism requires attestation of pricing. This partially makes W computable, though enforcement gaps remain.
Non cassable. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ regardless of source.

i: Note: Russia's domestic société civile is severely repressed. Bellona Foundation (Norway-based) — documents Russian environnemental issues. iStories, Novaya Gazeta Europe — independent Russian media operating from exile.
Gazprom — state gas monopoly. Rosneft — state oil company. Most Western majors (Shell, BP, ExxonMobil) have exited Russian operations since 2022.
G7 Russian Oil Price Cap Coalition — enforces $60/barrel cap through insurance and shipping services. EU sanctions packages (14+ rounds). OFAC (US), OFSI (UK) — sanctions enforcement.

Note: Russia's Federal Assembly (Federalnoye Sobraniye — State Duma 450 members, Federation Council 170 members) does not function as an independent legislature. International channels: G7/G20 (Russia suspended from G8), UN General Assembly, international sanctions coalitions.

RW.s: Small East African économie ($14.3B PIB). Agriculture à 24.6% PIB. Rentes forestières 3.97%. Services à 47.6%. Remarkable post-genocide development but autoritaire governance. Strong environnemental policy (plastic bag ban pioneer).

sectors.w: Rwanda's hillside agriculture on steep terrain causes erosion and dégradation des sols. Lorsque soil erosion data is linked to agricultural output per district, W devient calculable.
bp: Terracing and agroforestry expansion. Rwanda already has extensive terracing programs — extending to all cultivated hillsides with measurable soil retention targets breaks the intérêt chevauchant.
Per-district soil erosion monitoring linked to agricultural productivity data. Rwanda's strong data infrastructure supports this. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Hillside cultivation on steep slopes inherently risks erosion. Terracing and contour farming reduce but cannot eliminate dépendance du système.

i: REMA — the environnemental management authority. RAB — agricultural research and extension. Rwanda has strong institutional capacity relative to income level.
NAEB — manages agricultural export promotion (coffee, tea, pyrethrum). Rwanda's agricultural sector is organized through cooperatives and imihigo (performance contracts).
MINAGRI — agriculture ministry. RLMUA — land use authority. Rwanda's Vision 2050 includes environnemental sustainability targets.

Inteko Ishinga Amategeko (Parliament) — bicameral: Chamber of Deputies (80 members) and Senate (26 members). Note: Rwanda is effectively a one-party dominant state under RPF (Rwandan Patriotic Front). International channels: EAC (East African Community), AU, ICGLR.

SD.s: Conflict-affected économie ($49.7B PIB). Agriculture à 22.2% PIB. Mining 6.63% (gold). Rentes pétrolières 3.3% (reduced after South Sudan's independence). Civil war since April 2023 between SAF and RSF. Humanitarian catastrophe.

sectors.w: Sudan's artisanal gold exploitation minière uses mercury extensively and generates revenue streams funding armed groups. Lorsque gold revenue flows are traced, W devient calculable.
bp: International supply chain due diligence blocking conflict gold. Lorsque refiners verify provenance, the intérêt chevauchant between armed groups and gold revenues s'affaiblit.
Tracing gold flows from artisanal mines to international markets. LBMA (London Bullion Market Association) responsible sourcing standards provide the framework. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Artisanal exploitation minière uses mercury (Minamata Convention violation). Mercury-free processing methods exist but require investment.

i: Note: Sudan is in active civil war. No functioning domestic oversight exists. UN Panel of Experts — monitors sanctions and conflict dynamics. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — tracks artisanal exploitation minière and conflict resources.
Sudan's gold primarily flows through UAE (Dubai) trading hubs. LBMA — sets responsible sourcing standards for gold refiners worldwide.
LBMA — London Bullion Market Association, responsible sourcing standard-setter. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — can designate jurisdictions for gold laundering risks.

Note: Sudan's transitional government collapsed with the April 2023 war. No functioning legislature. International channels: UN Security Council, AU (African Union Peace and Security Council), IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Développerment).

SE.s: Advanced Nordic économie ($603.7B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 13% PIB. Mining 1% PIB (iron ore — LKAB is Europe's largest). Services à 66.4%. Strong environnemental regulations. membre de l'UE. Leading in green steel transition (HYBRIT).

sectors.w: Sweden's iron ore exploitation minière (LKAB) in northern Sweden affects Sami reindeer herding land and generates tailings. Lorsque par mine environnemental and indigenous impact data is published alongside production, W devient calculable.
bp: Full internalization of indigenous land-use costs and environnemental remediation. Lorsque LKAB pays for herding disruption and tailings management, l'intérêt chevauchant dans low-cost extraction s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental disclosure including impacts on Sami land use. LKAB publishes sustainability reports — extending to independently verified indigenous impact assessments makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. HYBRIT (Hydrogen Breakthrough Ironmaking Technology) — SSAB/LKAB/Vattenfall joint venture — is producing fossil-free steel, demonstrating that exploitation minière's downstream dépendance du système on carbon-intensive processing can be broken.
a: Signaler à Naturvårdsverket (Swedish Environmental Protection Agency)
Signaler à Bergsstaten (Mining Inspectorate of Sweden)

i: Naturvårdsverket — environnemental protection agency. Bergsstaten — the exploitation minière inspectorate, part of SGU (Sveriges geologiska undersökning — Geological Survey of Sweden). Sametinget — the Sami Parliament, representing indigenous Sami rights.

LKAB — d'État iron ore company, Europe's largest. SSAB — steel producer, partner in HYBRIT. Boliden — base metals exploitation minière and smelting company. HYBRIT — world's first fossil-free steel initiative.
Mark- och miljödomstolarna — specialized environnemental courts. Sweden's Minerals Act (Minerallagen) governs exploitation minière permits.
Riksdag — unicameral parliament, 349 members. Contact through riksdagen.se. Sweden is a monarchie constitutionnelle and membre de l'UE.

SG.s: Advanced city-state économie ($547.4B PIB). Services à 73% PIB. Industrie manufacturière à 16.3% (electronics, petrochemicals). No natural resources. Major petrochemical refining hub and global financial center. World's busiest transshipment port.

sectors.w: Singapore is a major petrochemical refining and trading hub — the 'Houston of Asia.' Lorsque refinery émissions and fossil fuel trading flows are disclosed per facility, W devient calculable.
bp: Carbon tax escalation (Singapore's taxe carbone rises to S$25/tCO₂ in 2024, S$45 in 2026, S$50-80 by 2030). Lorsque refining margins incorporate carbon costs, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unpriced émissions s'affaiblit.
Per-refinery émissions and trade-flow disclosure. MAS (Monetary Authority of Singapore) climate disclosure requirements extend to financial flows. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Petrochemical refining inherently emits. However, Singapore's investment in sustainable aviation fuel and green hydrogen can reduce dépendance du système over time.
a: Signaler à NEA (National Environment Agency)

i: NEA (National Environment Agency) — environnemental regulator. MAS (Monetary Authority of Singapore) — financial regulator with expanding climate disclosure mandate. SGX — Singapore Exchange, requires climate reporting.
Jurong Island — Singapore's petrochemical hub hosting Shell, ExxonMobil, and other major refineries. DBS, OCBC, UOB — Singapore's three major banks with significant fossil fuel financing exposure.
EMA (Energy Market Authority) — régulateur de l'énergie. Singapore Green Plan 2030 — national sustainability framework. Carbon tax administered by NEA under Carbon Pricing Act.

Parliament of Singapore — unicameral. Singapore is a parlementaire republic with dominant-party governance (PAP — People's Action Party). Note: limited political pluralism in practice. International channels: ASEAN (founding member), IMO, UNFCCC.

SI.s: Small membre de l'UE économie ($73B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 19.4% PIB. Services à 57.9%. Significant forest cover (60%+ of territory). membre de l'UE since 2004. Former Yugoslav republic with strong environnemental awareness.

sectors.w: Slovenia's intensive agriculture in lowland areas (Pomurje, Podravje) relies on monoculture grain and livestock. Lorsque soil and water quality data is linked to CAP payments, W devient calculable.
bp: EU CAP eco-scheme implementation. Lorsque subsidy payments require biodiversity and soil health measures, l'intérêt chevauchant dans exhaustive monoculture s'affaiblit.
Per-farm soil and water quality monitoring through ARSKTRP (Agency for Agricultural Markets and Rural Développerment). Rend W calculable.

Partiellement cassable. Grain monoculture and intensive livestock farming degrade soil and pollute groundwater. Organic farming and crop rotation reduce dependence — Slovenia already has 11%+ organic farming share.

Signaler à Inšpektorat za kmetijstvo (Agricultural Inspectorate)

i: ARSO — the Environment Agency of Slovenia. Umanotera — Slovenian Foundation for Sustainable Développerment. Focus — environnemental NGO.
Slovenia's agricultural sector is dominated by small family farms (average 7 hectares). Kmetijsko gozdarska zbornica (Chamber of Agriculture and Forestry) — industry association.
ARSKTRP (Agencija za kmetijske trge in razvoj podeželja) — manages CAP payments. Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Food — policy ministry.
Državni zbor (National Assembly) — 90 members. Državni svet (National Council) — 40 members, advisory role. membre de l'UE since 2004. Alpine Convention member. Contact through dz-rs.si.

SK.s: Central European membre de l'UE ($140.9B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 16.3% PIB — heavily auto-dependent (Volkswagen, Kia, Stellantis). Services à 60%. membre de l'UE since 2004. World's largest per-capita car producer.

sectors.w: Slovakia's food processing industry produces significant ultra-processed food for regional markets. Lorsque per-product health impact data (obesity, diabetes correlations) is disclosed alongside marketing spend, W devient calculable.
bp: EU front-of-pack labeling (Nutri-Score or equivalent). Lorsque consumers can identify ultra-processing, l'intérêt chevauchant dans opaque ingredient lists s'affaiblit.
Mandatory per-product health impact disclosure. Slovakia has rising obesity rates (20%+). Linking UPF consumption data to health outcomes makes W computable.
Non cassable. Ultra-processing by definition strips nutritional value and adds synthetic additives.

Slovakia's food retail is dominated by international chains (Lidl, Kaufland, Tesco, Billa). Slovenská poľnohospodárska a potravinárska komora — food industry chamber.
Ministry of Health — oversees dietary health policy. Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Développerment — food production policy.
Národná rada Slovenskej republiky (National Council) — unicameral, 150 members. membre de l'UE since 2004. Contact through nrsr.sk.

SN.s: West African économie ($32.8B PIB). Agriculture à 16.6% PIB. Mining 2.9% (phosphates, gold, zircon). Offshore oil and gas discoveries (Sangomar field — first oil expected). Pêche crucial for food security. Democracy with peaceful power transitions.

sectors.w: Senegal's fisheries face severe surpêche by foreign industrial fleets (EU, China) competing with artisanal pirogues. Lorsque per-vessel catch data is published alongside stock assessments, W devient calculable.
bp: Réformer fishing access agreements to limit foreign industrial fleet catch. Lorsque EU and Chinese DWF (Distant Water Fleet) quotas reflect stock sustainability, l'intérêt chevauchant dans surpêche s'affaiblit.
VMS (Vessel Monitoring System) data publication and per-vessel catch reporting. Senegal has invested in MCS (Monitoring, Control and Surveillance). Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Industrial trawling damages benthic habitats. Artisanal fishing with selective gear reduces dépendance du système.
a: Signaler à DPSP (Direction de la Protection et de la Surveillance des Pêches)
Documenter via ICSF (International Collective in Support of Fishworkers)

i: DPSP — fisheries protection directorate. CNSP — national fisheries surveillance center, operates patrol vessels. Senegal has one of West Africa's most developed fisheries monitoring systems.
Senegal's fisheries employ ~600,000 people. Artisanal pirogues (wooden boats) land ~80% of total catch. GAIPES — fish exporters association.
Ministry of Pêche and Maritime Economy. CRODT — oceanographic research center providing stock assessments. CSRP — sub-regional fisheries commission for West Africa.

Assemblée nationale du Sénégal — unicameral, 165 members. Senegal is a presidential republic with a strong democratic tradition (peaceful power transitions in 2000, 2012, 2024). Contact through assemblee-nationale.sn.

SO.s: Fragile state ($12B PIB). Limited economic data available. Rentes forestières 11.24% PIB (charcoal — major déforestation driver). Charcoal trade linked to Al-Shabaab financing. Ongoing conflict and state fragility.

sectors.w: Somalia's charcoal production devastates acacia forests and finances armed groups. Lorsque charcoal trade flows are traced and linked to déforestation data, W devient calculable.
bp: UN Security Council charcoal export ban enforcement (Resolution 2036). Lorsque the ban is effectively enforced, the intérêt chevauchant between Al-Shabaab revenue and charcoal trade s'affaiblit.
Satellite déforestation monitoring linked to charcoal trade routes. UN Monitoring Group has documented the flows. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Charcoal production from slow-growing acacia in arid Somali climate causes irreversible desertification.

i: UN Monitoring Group on Somalia and Eritrea — monitors sanctions including the charcoal ban. EU NAVFOR (Operation Atalanta) — maritime security operation. Note: domestic institutional capacity is extremely limited.
Somali charcoal is primarily exported to Gulf states via dhow networks. UAE has been the largest market. Some companies are promoting LPG as an alternative cooking fuel.
Federal Government of Somalia — limited authority outside Mogadishu. Federal Member States have varying governance capacity. UN sanctions framework provides the primary regulatory tool.

Federal Parliament of Somalia — bicameral: House of the People (275 members) and Upper House (54 members). Note: clan-based indirect elections, limited institutional capacity. International channels: UN, AU (ATMIS — AU Transition Mission in Somalia), IGAD.

SV.s: Small Central American économie ($35.4B PIB). Services à 61% PIB. Agriculture à 4.4%. Industrie manufacturière à 12%. Significant remittance dependence (~24% of PIB). Bitcoin adopted as legal tender (2021). State of exception since 2022.

sectors.w: El Salvador's sugarcane monoculture in coastal lowlands is linked to chronic kidney disease of unknown etiology (CKDu) among agricultural workers — one of the highest rates globally. Lorsque CKDu data is linked to agrochemical exposure per farm, W devient calculable.
bp: Agrochemical regulation and worker health monitoring. Lorsque sugarcane operations internalize CKDu health costs, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unregulated pesticide/herbicide use s'affaiblit.
Per-farm agrochemical use and worker health outcome data. El Salvador has the world's highest CKDu mortality. Linking these datasets makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Sugarcane monoculture depletes soils and concentrates agrochemical exposure. Crop diversification and organic practices reduce dépendance du système.

i: MARN — Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources. ISSS — social security institute. MAG — agriculture ministry. La Isla Network — international NGO documenting CKDu in Central America.
El Salvador's sugar industry is concentrated among a few large ingenios (sugar mills). CAMAGRO (Cámara Agropecuaria y Agroindustrial) — agricultural industry chamber.
Ministry of Health — responsible for CKDu response. MARN — environnemental regulation.
Asamblea Legislativa — unicameral, 84 members. Note: since 2021, executive dominance over legislature; limited separation of powers in practice. International channels: SICA, PAHO, ILO.

SY.s: War-devastated économie ($23.6B PIB). Agriculture à 43.1% PIB — unusually high due to industrial collapse. Rentes pétrolières 4.02%, gas 2.33%. Civil war since 2011 has destroyed infrastructure. Ongoing political transition (2024-2025).

sectors.w: Syria's oil infrastructure has been contested between factions (SDF/AANES controls northeastern fields, former regime controlled refineries). Lorsque oil revenue flows are transparently traced through reconstruction, W devient calculable.
bp: Post-conflict transparent governance of oil revenues. Lorsque reconstruction frameworks require accountable revenue management, l'intérêt chevauchant dans opaque extraction s'affaiblit.
Oil revenue flow transparence as condition of reconstruction assistance and sanctions relief. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Oil infrastructure has been extensively damaged — reconstruction offers opportunity to build in environnemental standards.

Note: Syria is undergoing political transition following the fall of the Assad regime (late 2024). New governance structures are forming. International channels: UN (Security Council, Special Envoy), Arab League (membership restored 2023), reconstruction donor conferences.

TD.s: Sahelian économie ($19.5B PIB). Rentes pétrolières 16.75% PIB. Agriculture à 37.7%. Rentes forestières 4.58%. One of the world's poorest countries. Military transitional government. Lake Chad shrinking (~90% since 1960s).

sectors.w: Chad's oil revenues flow through an opaque pipeline (Doba basin to Kribi, Cameroon). The World Bank's original revenue transparence mechanism (Collège de Contrôle) was dismantled. Lorsque oil revenue allocation is made transparent, W devient calculable.
bp: Restore oil revenue transparence mechanisms. The World Bank's original Chad-Cameroon pipeline project required transparent revenue allocation — this was dismantled. Rebuilding it breaks l'intérêt chevauchant dans opaque revenue flows.
Oil revenue flow disclosure through EITI. Chad was suspended from EITI in 2023 for governance failures. Restoring compliance makes W computable.
Partiellement cassable. Oil production in arid Sahel strains water resources. However, the Doba basin fields are maturing and production declining.

i: Note: Chad's société civile space is severely restricted. Publish What You Pay — international revenue transparence coalition. SWISSAID — has worked on Chad extractives governance.

CNPC — operates major oil fields after acquiring former ExxonMobil assets. Savannah Energy — British company that acquired former Chevron assets. Chad-Cameroon pipeline — 1,070 km pipeline to Atlantic coast.
SHT — state oil company. Ministry of Petroleum, Mines and Energy — oversees hydrocarbons. Chad was suspended from EITI in 2023.

Note: Military transitional government (Conseil Militaire de Transition, succeeded by elected government of Mahamat Déby, 2024). Conseil National de Transition — 93 members, appointed. International channels: AU, Lake Chad Basin Commission, CEMAC.

TG.s: Small West African économie ($10.7B PIB). Agriculture à 21.3% PIB. Mining 4.88% (phosphates — world's 5th largest producer). Services à 49.2%. Key transit port (Lomé). Governance concerns with limited political competition.

sectors.w: Togo's phosphate exploitation minière (SNPT — Société Nouvelle des Phosphates du Togo) generates cadmium-contaminated waste. Lorsque par mine environnemental data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: International cadmium standards in phosphate products. Lorsque export markets require cadmium limits, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unprocessed phosphate extraction s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring data including cadmium levels, water contamination, and land rehabilitation. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Phosphate rock contains cadmium. Cadmium removal technology exists and is commercially viable.

i: ANGE — national environnemental management agency. ITIE-Togo — Togo is an EITI candidate country.
SNPT (Société Nouvelle des Phosphates du Togo) — the state phosphate company, privatized then renationalized. Operates mines in the Hahotoé-Kpogamé region.
Ministry of Mines and Energy — exploitation minière oversight. ANGE — environnemental assessment authority.

Assemblée nationale du Togo — 113 members. Note: Togo has been governed by the Gnassingbé family since 1967; limited political competition. Constitutional change in 2024 shifted to parlementaire system. International channels: ECOWAS, AU, EITI.

TH.s: Major Southeast Asian économie ($526.5B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 24.3% PIB (automotive, electronics, food processing). Agriculture à 8.7%. Services à 59.2%. World's largest rice exporter. Significant petrochemical sector. Constitutional monarchy.

sectors.w: Thailand is a major UPF producer and exporter (CP Group — Charoen Pokphand — is one of Asia's largest food conglomerates). Lorsque per-product health impact data is linked to marketing and formulation, W devient calculable.
bp: Front-of-pack labeling reform. Thailand's existing traffic-light label (GDA) covers some nutrients — extending to ultra-processing indicators s'affaiblit l'intérêt chevauchant dans opaque formulation.
Per-product health outcome correlation data. Thailand's rising obesity and diabetes rates can be linked to UPF consumption patterns. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Ultra-processing by definition strips nutritional value.
