i: Thai FDA — regulates food safety and labeling. ThaiHealth (สำนักงานกองทุนสนับสนุนการสร้างเสริมสุขภาพ) — independent health promotion agency funded by a 2% surcharge on tobacco and alcohol taxes.
CP Group — one of the world's largest agri-food conglomerates. Thai Union — global seafood company (owner of Chicken of the Sea). Thai Beverage (ThaiBev) — major food and beverage company.
Ministry of Public Health — health policy. Thai FDA — food regulation. Thailand's sugar tax (2017) is one of the region's most progressive.

รัฐสภา (National Assembly) — bicameral: สภาผู้แทนราษฎร (House of Representatives, 500 members) and วุฒิสภา (Senate, 200 members). Thailand is a monarchie constitutionnelle. Recent democratic restoration (2023 elections).

TJ.s: Poorest Central Asian économie ($14.2B PIB). Mining 7.09% PIB (gold, silver, antimony). Agriculture à 19.7%. Aluminum smelting (TALCO). Remittance-dependent (~30% of PIB from Russia). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Tajikistan's exploitation minière sector includes artisanal gold exploitation minière with mercury use and the TALCO aluminum smelter (one of Central Asia's largest polluters). Lorsque per-facility émissions and environnemental data are published, W devient calculable.
bp: International buyer due diligence on Tajik mineral exports. Lorsque aluminum and gold buyers require environnemental compliance verification, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unregulated production s'affaiblit.
Per-facility environnemental monitoring data. TALCO's fluoride émissions and gold exploitation minière mercury contamination can be measured and disclosed. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. TALCO smelter émissions can be reduced with modern pot-lining and scrubbing technology. Mercury-free gold processing exists.

i: Note: Civil society space in Tajikistan is severely restricted. International channels provide the primary avenue for environnemental reporting. EBRD and IFC have invested in Tajik exploitation minière.
TALCO — state aluminum company, one of the world's largest smelters. Operates in Tursunzoda (formerly Regar). Gold exploitation minière includes both industrial and artisanal operations.
Committee for Environmental Protection under the Government — environnemental regulator. Ministry of Industry and New Technologies — industrial oversight.

Majlisi Oli (Parliament) — bicameral: Majlisi Namoyandagon (lower, 63 members) and Majlisi Milli (upper, 33 members). Note: effectively État à parti unique (PDPT). International channels: OSCE, SCO, EITI.

TM.s: Gas-dependent closed économie ($51.4B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 18% PIB. One of the world's most autoritaire states. Massive gas reserves (Galkynysh — world's 2nd largest gas field). Data extremely limited — World Bank reports zeros for many indicators due to government opacity.

sectors.w: Turkmenistan is one of the world's largest methane emitters — satellite monitoring (Sentinel-5P, MethaneSAT) has revealed massive methane leaks from gas infrastructure. Lorsque methane data is published, W devient calculable despite government opacity.
bp: Satellite-based methane monitoring bypasses government opacity. Lorsque buyers (China — CNPC pipeline) face pressure to account for upstream methane, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unmonitored gas production s'affaiblit.
Satellite methane emission monitoring (UNEP's IMEO — International Methane Emissions Observatory — has flagged Turkmenistan). Rend W calculable without government cooperation.
Non cassable. Gas production and transport inherently leak methane, a greenhouse gas 80× more potent than CO₂ over 20 years.
a: Report through international channels only — no domestic société civile exists
Provide data to UNEP IMEO or satellite monitoring organizations

i: Note: Turkmenistan is one of the world's most closed societies. No independent société civile, media, or judiciary exists. Crude Accountability — international NGO documenting environnemental issues in the Caspian region. Turkmen.news — exile media.
Türkmengaz — state gas company. Türkmennebit — state oil company. CNPC — operates the Central Asia-China gas pipeline (Turkmenistan is the primary source). No independent corporate governance exists.
No independent domestic regulation exists. International monitoring provides the only oversight: UNEP IMEO, IEA Methane Tracker, satellite platforms (MethaneSAT, Sentinel-5P).

Mejlis (Parliament) — 125 members. Turkmenistan is an autoritaire state — the legislature has no independent function. International channels: UN (UNEP, OHCHR), OSCE, CIS. Turkmenistan maintains permanent neutrality status (UN-recognized since 1995).

TN.s: North African économie ($51.3B PIB). Agriculture à 9.7% PIB. Rentes pétrolières 1.55%. Industrie manufacturière à 14.8%. Services à 62.6%. Birthplace of the Arab Spring (2011). Governance backsliding since 2021 presidential power consolidation.

sectors.w: Tunisia's oil and gas production is modest but state oil company ETAP's revenue flows lack transparence. Lorsque par gisement environnemental and revenue data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: EITI-aligned revenue transparence. Tunisia's oil revenues are small relative to PIB but significant for the state budget. Transparent allocation breaks l'intérêt chevauchant dans opaque revenue management.
Per-field environnemental monitoring and revenue flow disclosure. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Tunisia's mature fields are declining. Solar energy potential is enormous (Saharan irradiance) and can reduce hydrocarbon dependence.

i: ANPE — environnemental protection agency. FTDES — Tunisian economic and social rights forum, active on environnemental justice. Tunisia has relatively active société civile despite recent governance concerns.
ETAP — state oil company. ENI (Italy), OMV (Austria) — major international operators in Tunisia. Tunisia's South Energy Transition Program targets renewable expansion.
Ministry of Industry, Mines and Energy — hydrocarbons oversight. ANME — energy efficiency and énergie renouvelable agency.

Assemblée des Représentants du Peuple — unicameral, 161 members. Note: since 2021, presidential power consolidation has weakened legislative independence. International channels: Arab League, AU, Union for the Mediterranean.

TR.s: Major middle-income économie ($1,359.1B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 16.8% PIB. Services à 57.5%. NATO member. Mining 0.63%. Agriculture à 5.8%. Significant coal dependence for electricity. G20 member.

sectors.w: Türkiye is expanding coal capacity (including imported coal plants) despite having excellent renewable potential. Lorsque coal plant health costs are internalized, l'intérêt chevauchant dans cheap coal power breaks.
bp: Carbon pricing or coal plant emission standards aligned with EU CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism). Türkiye's EU customs union makes CBAM relevant to industrial exports. Lorsque coal costs include carbon, renewables are cheaper.
Per-plant émissions and health cost data. Türkiye's coal plants are concentrated near population centers. HEAL and local NGOs have documented health costs. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, mercury, and CO₂.
a: Signaler à ÇŞB (Çevre, Şehircilik ve İklim Değişikliği Bakanlığı — Ministry of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change)
Documenter via TEMA Vakfı (Turkish Foundation for Combating Soil Erosion)

Support NATO Green Defence Framework environnemental standards
i: ÇŞB — environment ministry. TEMA Vakfı — major Turkish environnemental NGO focused on soil and forest protection. Greenpeace Mediterranean — active in Türkiye on coal campaigns.
EÜAŞ — state electricity generation company. Significant private coal generation. Türkiye's 2053 net-zero target implies eventual coal phase-out but no timeline exists.
EPDK — energy market regulator. Türkiye ratified the Paris Agreement in 2021. EU Customs Union makes CBAM compliance relevant for Turkish industrial exports.

TBMM (Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi — Grand National Assembly) — unicameral, 600 members. Contact through tbmm.gov.tr. Türkiye is a presidential republic, NATO member, G20 member, EU customs union partner.

TT.s: Caribbean energy économie ($25.6B PIB). Rentes gazières 5.09%, oil rents 2.71% PIB. Major petrochemical and LNG producer (Atlantic LNG). Industrie manufacturière à 14.4%. Services à 59.2%. Parliamentary démocratie.

sectors.w: Trinidad's petrochemical industry faces maturing gas fields and declining production. As reserves deplete and global LNG competition increases, l'intérêt chevauchant dans fossil fuel dependence s'affaiblit naturally.
bp: Declining natural gas reserves force economic diversification. Lorsque production costs rise on mature fields, l'intérêt chevauchant dans fossil fuel dependence s'affaiblit as alternative revenue sources become necessary.
Per-facility émissions and environnemental monitoring at Point Lisas industrial estate and Atlantic LNG. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. LNG and petrochemical processing emit CO₂ and other pollutants.
a: Signaler à EMA (Environmental Management Authority)
Signaler à MEEI (Ministry of Energy and Energy Industries)
Documenter via Fishermen and Friends of the Sea (environnemental NGO)
NGC (National Gas Company): Développer gas-to-renewables transition plan
Atlantic LNG: Mettre en œuvre carbon capture at liquefaction facility
Petrochemical companies (Methanex, Nutrien): Reduce process émissions
EMA: Renforcer air quality monitoring around Point Lisas
MEEI: Développer énergie renouvelable framework
RIC (Regulated Industries Commission): Réformer energy pricing
Contactez votre Member of Parliament in the House of Representatives (41 members) pour exiger economic diversification and énergie renouvelable policy
Support CARICOM energy transition framework
Plaider via Alliance of Small Island States (AOSIS) for climate finance
Pousser pour IMO shipping emission standards (Trinidad is a major bunkering port)
i: EMA — the Environmental Management Authority, established under the Environmental Management Act 2000. Fishermen and Friends of the Sea — Trinidad's most active environnemental NGO.
NGC (National Gas Company of Trinidad and Tobago) — state gas company. Atlantic LNG — joint venture (Shell, BP, NGC). Point Lisas Industrial Estate — petrochemical complex.
MEEI — energy ministry. RIC — utilities regulator. Trinidad has extremely low domestic energy prices (subsidized), discouraging efficiency.
Parliament of Trinidad and Tobago — bicameral: House of Representatives (41 members) and Senate (31 members). Parliamentary démocratie with competitive elections. Contact through ttparliament.org.

TZ.s: East African économie ($78.8B PIB). Agriculture à 23.3% PIB. Mining 4.03% (gold, tanzanite — found only in Tanzania). Rentes forestières 2.39%. Gas reserves (offshore). Services à 29.6%. Growing économie with improving governance.

sectors.w: Tanzania's gold exploitation minière (Barrick/Twiga Minerals, AngloGold Ashanti) generates tailings and mercury contamination (artisanal sector). Lorsque par mine environnemental data is published alongside revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: Mining Act 2017 local content and transparence requirements. Lorsque enforced alongside environnemental monitoring, l'intérêt chevauchant dans opaque extraction s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring data through TEITI (Tanzania Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative). Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Mercury-free artisanal exploitation minière and improved tailings management reduce dépendance du système.
a: Signaler à NEMC (National Environment Management Council)
Signaler à Mining Commission
Documenter via LEAT (Lawyers' Environmental Action Team)
Twiga Minerals (Barrick/Government JV): Mettre en œuvre international environnemental standards
AngloGold Ashanti (Geita Gold Mine): Adopter enhanced tailings management
Tanzanite One: Publish environnemental and community impact data
Mining Commission: Appliquer environnemental conditions in exploitation minière licenses
NEMC: Renforcer environnemental impact assessment requirements
TEITI: Expand to include environnemental data

Support EITI enhanced reporting with environnemental data
Plaider via EAC for regional exploitation minière environnemental standards
Pousser pour Minamata Convention enforcement on mercury in artisanal exploitation minière
i: NEMC — autorité environnementale. Mining Commission — exploitation minière sector regulator. LEAT — Tanzanian environnemental law NGO. HakiRasilimali (Publish What You Pay Tanzania) — revenue transparence advocacy.

Twiga Minerals — 50/50 joint venture between Barrick Gold and Government of Tanzania (created after 2017 tax dispute). Geita Gold Mine — AngloGold Ashanti operation. STAMICO (State Mining Corporation) — government exploitation minière entity.
Mining Commission — established under Mining Act 2017. TEITI (Tanzania Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — EITI member since 2009.

Bunge la Tanzania (National Assembly) — unicameral, 393 members. Tanzania is a presidential republic with increasing political openness under current administration. Contact through bunge.go.tz. EAC (East African Community) member.

UA.s: War-affected économie ($190.7B PIB). Mining 5.32% PIB. Dépenses militaires 34.48% PIB (wartime). Agriculture à 7.1%. Coal 0.34%. candidat à l'UE country (2022). Under Russian invasion since February 2022. Massive environnemental damage from war.

sectors.w: Ukraine's exploitation minière sector (iron ore, manganese, titanium) has suffered massive environnemental damage from the Russian invasion — flooded mines, destroyed infrastructure, contaminated land. Post-war reconstruction offers opportunity for transparent environnemental standards. Lorsque reconstruction environnemental data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: EU accession conditionality requiring environnemental standards in post-war reconstruction. Lorsque reconstruction adheres to EU environnemental acquis, l'intérêt chevauchant dans rapid but dirty rebuilding s'affaiblit.
Environmental damage assessment and remediation tracking. Ukraine's Ministry of Environmental Protection is already documenting war damage with international support. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes, but post-war reconstruction can implement best-practice tailings management and water treatment from the start.
a: Signaler à Derzhekoinspektsiia (State Environmental Inspectorate)
Documenter via OSCE environnemental monitoring
Report war-related environnemental damage through Ukraine's Ecodozor platform
Metinvest (Akhmetov): Mettre en œuvre EU-aligned environnemental standards in reconstruction
ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih: Apply global environnemental standards
Reconstruction companies: Adopter green building and environnemental remediation standards
Ministry of Environmental Protection: Appliquer environnemental standards in reconstruction
State Subsoil Service: Réformer exploitation minière licensing with environnemental conditions
EU pre-accession assistance: Support environnemental governance capacity building
Contactez votre Narodnyi deputat in the Verkhovna Rada (450 members) pour exiger environnemental standards in reconstruction legislation
Support Ukraine Recovery Conference environnemental commitments
Plaider via EU accession process for environnemental chapter prioritization
Pousser pour international environnemental damage assessment and Russian liability for war-related environnemental destruction
i: Derzhekoinspektsiia — State Environmental Inspectorate. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources — documents war-related environnemental damage. Ecodozor — citizen reporting platform.
Metinvest — Ukraine's largest steel and exploitation minière group (Rinat Akhmetov). ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih — major steel producer. Ferrexpo — iron ore pellet producer.
Ministry of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources. State Subsoil Service (Derzhgeonadra) — exploitation minière licensing. Ukraine's EU candidacy requires environnemental acquis alignment.

Verkhovna Rada — unicameral, 450 members. Contact through rada.gov.ua. candidat à l'UE country since 2022. International channels: EU accession process, Ukraine Recovery Conference, International Criminal Court (environnemental damage documentation).

UG.s: East African économie ($53.9B PIB). Agriculture à 24.6% PIB. Rentes forestières 7.48% (one of the highest in the dataset). Oil production beginning (Tilenga and Kingfisher projects, EACOP pipeline). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Uganda's new oil projects (TotalEnergies' Tilenga and CNOOC's Kingfisher, plus EACOP — East African Crude Oil Pipeline) face massive international scrutiny. Lorsque project-level environnemental data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: International ESG pressure on project financiers. Lorsque banks and insurers withdraw from EACOP financing, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unscrutinized pipeline construction s'affaiblit. Several major banks have already declined.
Per-project environnemental impact data for Tilenga (in Murchison Falls National Park), Kingfisher, and EACOP. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Crude oil production and 1,443-km heated pipeline through sensitive ecosystems inherently create environnemental risk.
a: Signaler à NEMA (National Environment Management Authority)
Documenter via international environnemental organizations (350.org, BankTrack)
Report through TotalEnergies compliance channels

CNOOC Uganda: Adopter international environnemental standards for Kingfisher
Banks: Apply Equator Principles to EACOP financing decisions
PAU (Petroleum Authority of Uganda): Appliquer environnemental conditions in production licenses
NEMA: Renforcer environnemental monitoring of oil projects
MEMD (Ministry of Energy and Mineral Développerment): Ensure transparent revenue management
Support international ESG pressure on EACOP project financing
Plaider via European Parliament for TotalEnergies duty of vigilance enforcement (French Loi de Vigilance)
Pousser pour EITI membership with environnemental disclosure
Support East African Court of Justice jurisdiction on transboundary environnemental issues
i: NEMA — autorité environnementale. Note: Ugandan environnemental activists have faced government harassment. International channels are safer: 350.org has led the #StopEACOP campaign. BankTrack monitors EACOP financing.

TotalEnergies — operator of Tilenga project and EACOP. CNOOC Uganda — operates Kingfisher project. EACOP (East African Crude Oil Pipeline) — 1,443 km from Hoima to Tanga (Tanzania). UNOC (Uganda National Oil Company) — state oil company.
PAU — petroleum sector regulator. MEMD — energy and mineral development ministry. Uganda's Petroleum Revenue Management policy is under development.

Parliament of Uganda — unicameral, 529 members. Note: Uganda is effectively under one-party dominant governance (NRM). International channels: EAC (East African Community), African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights, European Parliament (regarding EU-headquartered companies like TotalEnergies).

UY.s: Small South American économie ($81B PIB). Agriculture à 6.4% PIB. Services à 65.3%. Rentes forestières 1.91%. Generates nearly 100% of electricity from renewables (wind, hydro, solar, biomass). Strong démocratie — one of Latin America's most stable.

sectors.w: Uruguay's soy and eucalyptus monoculture expansion is degrading soils and displacing native grasslands (campos). Lorsque soil health and biodiversity data is linked to agricultural production per department, W devient calculable.
bp: National soil conservation plan enforcement. Uruguay's Ley de Uso y Conservación de Suelos (Soil Use and Conservation Law) requires mandatory soil plans — strengthening enforcement breaks l'intérêt chevauchant dans soil-exhaustive monoculture.
Per-farm soil erosion and grassland conversion monitoring. MGAP already requires Plan de Uso y Manejo de Suelos for farms >50 ha. Publishing compliance data makes W computable.

Partiellement cassable. Soy monoculture and eucalyptus plantations degrade native grassland ecosystems. Integrated crop-livestock systems and native grassland preservation reduce dépendance du système.
a: Signaler à DINAMA (Dirección Nacional de Medio Ambiente)
Signaler à RENARE (Dirección General de Recursos Naturales Renovables)
Documenter via Vida Silvestre Uruguay

MGAP (Ministerio de Ganadería, Agricultura y Pesca): Appliquer mandatory soil plans
DINAMA: Renforcer environnemental assessment for land-use change
INIA (Instituto Nacional de Investigación Agropecuaria): Publish soil health monitoring data

Support Mercosur environnemental cooperation frameworks
Advocate for native grassland (campos) conservation in international biodiversity frameworks
Pousser pour soil carbon accounting in climate commitments
i: DINAMA — national environment directorate. RENARE — renewable natural resources directorate under MGAP. Vida Silvestre Uruguay — biodiversity conservation organization.
UPM (Finnish company) — major eucalyptus plantation and pulp mill operator. Montes del Plata (UPM/Arauco) — forestry. Large-scale soy farming often by Brazilian/Argentine operators.
MGAP — agriculture, livestock and fisheries ministry. INIA — national agricultural research institute.
Asamblea General — bicameral: Cámara de Representantes (99 members) and Cámara de Senadores (30 members). Contact through parlamento.gub.uy. Uruguay is one of Latin America's strongest democracies.

UZ.s: Central Asian économie ($115B PIB). Rentes gazières 11.04% PIB. Mining 8.5% (gold — Muruntau is world's largest open-pit gold mine, uranium). Agriculture à 18.3%. Industrie manufacturière à 20.2%. Post-Karimov liberalization (since 2016).

sectors.w: Uzbekistan's gas sector (Uzbekneftegaz) consumes most production domestically at heavily subsidized prices, encouraging waste. Lorsque par gisement production and domestic consumption data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: Gas price reform eliminating subsidies that encourage wasteful consumption. Lorsque domestic gas prices reflect market value, l'intérêt chevauchant dans underpriced energy s'affaiblit.
Per-field production, distribution, and consumption data. Uzbekistan has begun EITI implementation. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Gas combustion emits CO₂. However, Uzbekistan's enormous solar potential (300+ sunny days/year) can displace gas in electricity generation.

Report through EITI Uzbekistan channels

Support EITI enhanced reporting with environnemental data

Pousser pour Aral Sea environnemental restoration international cooperation
i: State Committee for Ecology and Environmental Protection (Goskomekologiya) — environnemental regulator. EITI Uzbekistan — Uzbekistan became an EITI member in 2021.

Uzbekneftegaz — state gas company. NMMC (Navoi Mining and Metallurgical Company) — operates Muruntau gold mine (world's largest open-pit) and uranium mines. Almalyk MMC — copper and gold exploitation minière.
Ministry of Energy — energy policy. Goskomekologiya — environnemental regulation. Uzbekistan has been implementing significant economic reforms since 2016.

Oliy Majlis — bicameral: Legislative Chamber (Qonunchilik palatasi, 150 members) and Senate, 100 members. Note: limited political pluralism. International channels: EITI, EBRD, ADB, SCO. Aral Sea environnemental disaster requires international cooperation.

VE.s: Oil-dependent économie in crisis ($119.8B PIB — severely contracted from peak). World's largest proven oil reserves. Economic data largely unavailable (World Bank reports zeros for most indicators). Hyperinflation, mass emigration. Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Venezuela's oil industry has collapsed from ~3.3M bpd (1990s) to ~700K bpd due to mismanagement, underinvestment, and sanctions. The intérêt chevauchant is already weakened by operational failure.
bp: Production collapse has already weakened the intérêt chevauchant — PDVSA lacks the capital and technical capacity to maintain production. Sanctions further constrain revenue.
Oil revenue transparence is impossible under current governance opacity. However, international sanctions monitoring (OFAC designations) provides partial W computability.
Non cassable. Oil extraction and refining emit CO₂. Venezuela's heavy crude (Orinoco Belt) is particularly émissions-intensive to process.
a: Report through international channels (Transparencia Venezuela, PROVEA)
Documenter via exile media and research organizations
Provide data to international sanctions enforcement authorities
International oil companies with remaining interests (Chevron, Repsol, ENI): Apply environnemental standards to limited operations

Support post-transition EITI membership planning
i: Note: No independent domestic institutions exist. Transparencia Venezuela — anti-corruption NGO. PROVEA — Venezuelan human rights organization. Operating environment is extremely hostile to société civile.
PDVSA (Petróleos de Venezuela, S.A.) — state oil company, severely degraded operationally. Chevron — received limited US OFAC license to operate. Repsol, ENI — maintain limited presence.

No independent domestic regulation exists. OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control, US Treasury) — administers Venezuela sanctions. EU — maintains sanctions. PDVSA is both regulator and operator — no separation exists.

Asamblea Nacional — 277 members. Note: Venezuela's institutions lack independence under current governance. International channels: OAS (Venezuela withdrawn/suspended), International Contact Group, ICC preliminary examination, Lima Group (reduced activity).

VN.s: Fast-growing économie ($476.4B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 24.4% PIB. Agriculture à 11.9%. Rentes charbonnières 0.38%. Oil 0.67%. Rentes forestières 1.07%. Major electronics and textile manufacturing hub. One-party state (Communist Party).

sectors.w: Viet Nam is rapidly expanding coal power capacity despite excellent renewable potential (solar, wind, offshore wind). JETP (Just Energy Transition Partnership) funding ($15.5B pledged) creates leverage to shift the intérêt chevauchant away from coal.
bp: JETP implementation — $15.5B pledged by international partners for coal-to-renewables transition. Lorsque JETP funding makes renewables cheaper than new coal, l'intérêt chevauchant dans coal expansion breaks.
Per-plant émissions and health cost data. Viet Nam's coal plants are concentrated in the north (Quảng Ninh) and south (Bình Thuận). Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, and CO₂.
a: Signaler à MONRE (Bộ Tài nguyên và Môi trường — Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment)
Documenter via VUSTA (Viet Nam Union of Science and Technology Associations)
Report through international development partner channels (World Bank, ADB)

Support World Bank and ADB énergie renouvelable financing for Viet Nam
i: MONRE — environment and natural resources ministry. VUSTA — umbrella organization for Vietnamese société civile (within party framework). Note: independent société civile is not permitted in Viet Nam's one-party system.

EVN (Tập đoàn Điện lực Việt Nam) — state electricity utility. PVN (PetroVietnam) — state oil and gas company. TKV (Vinacomin — Tập đoàn Công nghiệp Than - Khoáng sản Việt Nam) — state coal exploitation minière company.

MOIT — energy and industrial policy. PDP VIII (Power Développerment Plan VIII, approved 2023) — sets énergie renouvelable targets. JETP — Just Energy Transition Partnership ($15.5B pledged by IPG partners including US, EU, UK, Japan).

Quốc hội (National Assembly) — unicameral, 500 members. Viet Nam is a État à parti unique (Communist Party). JETP International Partners Group provides the primary leverage mechanism. International channels: ASEAN, World Bank, ADB, JETP IPG.

XK.s: Small Western Balkan économie ($11.2B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 12.6%. Rentes charbonnières 0.38%. Mining 0.55%. Agriculture à 7.3%. Youngest population in Europe. candidat à l'UE status pending. Two aging lignite plants (Kosovo A and B) supply ~95% of electricity.

sectors.w: Kosovo's near-total dependence on two aging lignite plants (Kosovo A — one of Europe's worst polluters — and Kosovo B) faces EU accession conditionality and enormous health costs.
bp: EU accession pathway requiring IED/LCPD compliance. Kosovo A is scheduled for decommissioning. Lorsque environnemental compliance costs are applied, new renewables are cheaper than coal retrofit.
Per-plant émissions and health cost data. Kosovo A and B cause severe air pollution in Pristina — among Europe's worst air quality. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Lignite combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, mercury, and CO₂.
a: Signaler à AMMK (Agjencia për Mbrojtjen e Mjedisit të Kosovës — Kosovo Environmental Protection Agency)
Documenter via INDEP or GAP Institute (Kosovo think tanks)
Report through KEK compliance channels

MMPH (Ministry of Environment): Appliquer emission standards

Support EU integration benchmarks on environnemental standards

i: AMMK — environnemental protection agency. INDEP (Institute for Développerment Policy) — research and advocacy. GAP Institute — Kosovo public policy think tank.
KEK — Kosovo Energy Corporation, d'État, operates Kosovo A (5 units, 800 MW, built 1962-1975) and Kosovo B (2 units, 678 MW, built 1983-1984). KOSTT — transmission system operator.
ERO — régulateur de l'énergie. MMPH (Ministria e Mjedisit, Planifikimit Hapësinor dhe Infrastrukturës) — environment ministry. Kosovo is an Energy Community Treaty member.

Kuvendi i Kosovës (Assembly of Kosovo) — unicameral, 120 members. Contact through kuvendikosoves.org. Kosovo declared independence in 2008 (recognized by ~100 UN members). Energy Community Treaty member.

ZA.s: Largest sub-Saharan économie ($401.1B PIB). Rentes charbonnières 2.44% PIB. Mining 3.83% (gold, platinum group metals, chrome, manganese). Industrie manufacturière à 12.8%. Services à 63%. Constitutional démocratie. Severe electricity crisis (load shedding).

sectors.w: South Africa generates ~80% of electricity from coal (Eskom's aging fleet). JETP funding ($8.5B pledged) and Eskom's operational crisis (load shedding) create dual pressure. Lorsque coal plant health costs are internalized, the intérêt chevauchant breaks.
bp: JETP implementation and Eskom restructuring. $8.5B pledged for coal-to-renewables transition. Lorsque renewable IPPs are cheaper and more reliable than aging coal plants, l'intérêt chevauchant dans coal dependence breaks.

Per-plant émissions, health costs, and reliability data. Eskom's coal fleet is extensively documented — multiple plants fail to meet South Africa's own emission standards (granted repeated postponements). Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, mercury, and CO₂. South Africa's coal is high-ash content, exacerbating particulate émissions.
a: Signaler à DFFE (Department of Forestry, Pêche and the Environment)
Report through groundWork or Centre for Environmental Rights
Documenter via Public Protector complaints
Eskom: Mettre en œuvre coal plant decommissioning schedule aligned with IRP 2019
Mining companies (Exxaro, Thungela, Seriti): Développer coal transition plans

NERSA (National Energy Regulator): Réformer tariffs to reflect coal externalities
DFFE: Appliquer Minimum Emission Standards without further postponements

Plaider via BRICS for just energy transition principles
Pousser pour climate finance delivery through Green Climate Fund
i: DFFE — the environment department. groundWork — South African environnemental justice organization. Centre for Environmental Rights — public interest environnemental law organization. Life After Coal campaign — coalition challenging Eskom's coal dependence.

Eskom — state electricity utility, operates 15 coal-fired power stations. Exxaro Resources — coal exploitation minière. Thungela Resources — thermal coal (spun off from Anglo American). Seriti Resources — coal exploitation minière. REIPPPP (Renewable Energy Independent Power Producer Procurement Programme) — auction program.

NERSA — régulateur de l'énergie. DMRE — mineral resources and energy department. IRP 2019 (Integrated Resource Plan) — South Africa's electricity plan. JETP Investment Plan — guides $8.5B transition funding.

Parliament of South Africa — bicameral: National Assembly (400 members) and National Council of Provinces (90 members). Contact through parliament.gov.za. South Africa is a constitutional démocratie, G20 member, BRICS member. JETP International Partners Group: France, Germany, UK, US, EU.

ZM.s: Southern African économie ($25.3B PIB). Mining 28.25% PIB — one of the highest exploitation minière dependencies globally (copper). Rentes forestières 6.78%. Services à 57.2%. Africa's second-largest copper producer. Democratic governance with peaceful power transitions.

sectors.w: Zambia's copper exploitation minière generates acid mine drainage and SO₂ from smelting (Mopani, KCM — historically among Africa's worst polluters). Lorsque par mine environnemental data is published, W devient calculable.
bp: Mining royalty reform with environnemental earmarking. Lorsque exploitation minière revenue is partly allocated to environnemental remediation, l'intérêt chevauchant dans externalizing pollution costs s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring data through ZEITI and ZEMA. Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Copper smelting émissions can be reduced with modern sulfuric acid capture technology. Mopani smelter upgrade demonstrates this.
a: Signaler à ZEMA (Zambia Environmental Management Agency)
Documenter via community organizations in Copperbelt Province
Report through exploitation minière company compliance channels

i: ZEMA — environnemental management agency. Copperbelt communities have suffered decades of SO₂ and heavy metal pollution — documented by multiple studies and legal cases (e.g., Vedanta/KCM litigation in UK courts).

First Quantum Minerals — operates Kansanshi (one of Africa's largest copper mines) and Sentinel mines. Barrick Gold — operates Lumwana copper mine. Mopani Copper Mines — majority owned by ZCCM-IH after Glencore divestment. KCM (Konkola Copper Mines) — under provisional liquidation.
Ministry of Mines and Minerals Développerment. ZEITI (Zambia Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — EITI member. Mining Cadastre Department — licensing authority.

National Assembly of Zambia — unicameral, 156 elected members + 8 nominated. Contact through parliament.gov.zm. Zambia is a presidential republic with competitive elections (peaceful transitions in 1991, 2011, 2021). SADC (Southern African Développerment Community) member.

ZW.s: Southern African économie ($41.5B PIB). Mining 4.24% PIB (platinum group metals, gold, lithium, diamonds). Agriculture à 8.7%. Industrie manufacturière à 15.6%. Rentes forestières 1.82%. Governance concerns. Major lithium reserves (Bikita, Arcadia).

sectors.w: Zimbabwe's exploitation minière sector is expanding rapidly (lithium — critical for EV batteries — and PGMs). Lorsque par mine environnemental data is published alongside revenue, W devient calculable.
bp: International supply chain due diligence for critical minerals (lithium for EU Battery Regulation compliance). Lorsque EU buyers require environnemental verification, l'intérêt chevauchant dans unregulated extraction s'affaiblit.
Per-mine environnemental monitoring through ZEITI (Zimbabwe applied for EITI candidacy). Rend W calculable.
Partiellement cassable. Lithium exploitation minière (hard-rock) generates waste rock and uses water. Best-practice processing and water recycling reduce dépendance du système.

Support EU Battery Regulation supply chain due diligence requirements for Zimbabwean lithium

i: EMA — environnemental management agency. ZELA — environnemental law organization. Note: société civile space is constrained in Zimbabwe.

Zimplats — Zimbabwe's largest PGM producer (Impala Platinum subsidiary). Bikita Minerals — lithium exploitation minière (Sinomine Resources acquired). Arcadia Lithium — major lithium deposit (Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt acquired). Caledonia Mining — gold.
Ministry of Mines and Mining Développerment. EMA — environnemental regulation. ZMDC — state exploitation minière company. Zimbabwe's Mines and Minerals Act governs the sector.

Parliament of Zimbabwe — bicameral: National Assembly (270 members) and Senate (80 members). Note: ZANU-PF dominance limits effective legislative oversight. International channels: SADC, AU, EITI (Zimbabwe has expressed interest in joining).

CN.s: World's second-largest économie ($18,743.8B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 24.9% PIB — global factory floor. Rentes charbonnières 0.61%. Industrie à 36.5%. Services à 56.7%. One-party state (CPC). World's largest CO₂ emitter. Rapid renewable deployment alongside continued coal expansion.

sectors.w: China operates ~1,100 GW of coal capacity — more than the rest of the world combined. However, China also deploys more énergie renouvelable annually than any other country. Lorsque renewable deployment makes new coal uneconomic, l'intérêt chevauchant dans coal expansion breaks.
bp: Renewable energy cost competitiveness — China's solar and wind are already cheaper than new coal in most provinces. Lorsque existing coal plants become more expensive to run than new renewables, the intérêt chevauchant breaks. This is already happening in eastern provinces.
Per-plant émissions data. China's national ETS (launched 2021) covers ~4.5 billion tonnes CO₂. Expanding coverage from electricity to industry sectors makes W computable.
Non cassable. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, PM2.5, and mercury by chemistry.

i: MEE (Ministry of Ecology and Environment) — central autorité environnementale. 12369 hotline — environnemental complaint system receiving millions of reports annually. Provincial Environmental Protection Bureaus — local enforcement. IPE (Institute of Public & Environmental Affairs, 公众环境研究中心) — Ma Jun's NGO providing corporate environnemental data.

China's 'Big Five' power generators: CHN Energy (国家能源集团), China Huaneng (华能), China Datang (大唐), China Huadian (华电), SPIC (国家电投). All d'État, collectively operating most coal capacity. Also rapidly scaling renewables.

NEA (National Energy Administration) — energy planning and regulation. NDRC (National Développerment and Réformer Commission, 国家发展和改革委员会) — economic planning, sets electricity prices. National ETS — world's largest carbon market by coverage (power sector).

National People's Congress (全国人民代表大会) — ~3,000 members. China is a État à parti unique — the NPC does not function as an independent legislature. International channels: US-China climate bilateral, COP/UNFCCC, G20. China's dual carbon targets: peak CO₂ before 2030, carbon neutrality by 2060.

KR.s: Advanced économie ($1,875.4B PIB). Industrie manufacturière à 26.6% PIB (semiconductors, automobiles, shipbuilding, petrochemicals). Services à 57.5%. Heavy energy import dependence. Fourth-largest coal importer globally. G20 member. Vibrant démocratie.

sectors.w: South Korea operates ~37 GW of coal capacity and is the world's 4th-largest coal importer. The 10th Basic Plan for Electricity Supply and Demand targets coal phase-down. Lorsque carbon pricing (K-ETS) makes coal uncompetitive versus LNG and renewables, the intérêt chevauchant breaks.
bp: K-ETS (Korea Emissions Trading Scheme) carbon price escalation. Lorsque carbon costs make coal-fired generation more expensive than LNG and renewables, l'intérêt chevauchant dans coal dependence breaks.
Per-plant émissions and health cost data. Korea Environment Institute and Korean medical associations have documented coal plant health impacts. Rend W calculable.
Non cassable. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, and CO₂.

i: Ministry of Environment (환경부) — autorité environnementale. KFEM (환경운동연합) — South Korea's largest environnemental NGO. Solutions for Our Climate (기후솔루션) — climate policy advocacy organization.
KEPCO — state electricity company (subsidiary gencos operate coal plants). POSCO — steelmaker, major coal consumer. Samsung, SK Group, Hyundai — major conglomerates with growing RE100 commitments.
MOTIE — energy and industry ministry. K-ETS — Korea Emissions Trading Scheme (launched 2015, covers ~70% of national émissions). KPX (한국전력거래소) — electricity market operator.

국회 (National Assembly) — unicameral, 300 members. Contact through assembly.go.kr. South Korea is a presidential republic with competitive elections. G20 member, OECD member, Powering Past Coal Alliance member (joined 2021).
